title: OSPSuite Version 10 released
body_text:
It is with great pleasure that we are announcing today the release of version 10 of the Open Systems Pharmacology Suite.
You can download the version here
As always, do not hesitate to post your feedback or suggestions in the forum.
Release Notes for the Open Systems Pharmacology Software Suite 10
New features
New population templates (Chronic Kidney Disease) added
3 CKD population templates added (based on the publication Malik PRV, Yeung CHT, Advani U, Dije S, Edginton AN. A Physiological Approach to Pharmacokinetics in Chronic Kidney Disease. J Clin Pharmacol 2020. 60 Suppl 1: S52-S62. doi: 10.1002/jcph.1713):

CKD - Stage 3: defined as patients with GFR of 31 - 60 [mL/min/1.73m²]
CKD - Stage 4: defined as patients with GFR of 16 - 30 [mL/min/1.73m²]
CKD - Stage 5: defined as patients with GFR of    1 - 15 [mL/min/1.73m²]

Each population includes 1000 individuals aged 18-80 years old, 50% male, based on European population (ICRP)

OSP Platform qualification library and PBPK Models Library
PBPK Models library extended
New PBPK models added:

Amikacin
Fluconazole
Montelukast
Raltegravir
Sufentanil
Vancomycin

Model building process and model quality of every new PBPK model is documented in the corresponding model evaluation report.
New releases of OSP Platform qualification library and PBPK Models Library
As with every new OSP Suite release, ALL platform qualification reports and model evaluation reports have been recreated with the new version of the OSP Suite and the latest version of the OSP Qualification Framework:

OSP Qualification Reports library
OSP-PBPK-Model-Library

Modeling of Protein expressions in PK-Sim redesigned and extended
See the documentation for details.


More flexible protein localization settings

Enzymes/Binding partners: introduction of fraction expressed in different compartments
Transporters: introduction of fraction expressed apical/basolateral (Kidney/Liver/GI) and fraction expressed blood brain barrier/tissue (brain)


Introduction of Initial Protein Concentration as explicit parameter

See Localizations and initial concentrations of enzymes and Localizations, directions, and initial concentrations of transport proteins for detailed descriptions.


New transporter types:

Plasma <=> Blood Cells
Plasma <=> Interstitial
Bidirectional



Transport direction (Influx/Efflux/Bidirectional/Pgp) can be set in each organ independently



Observed data import in PK-Sim and MoBi redesigned and extended
See the documentation for details.


Better support for "Nonmem-like" data formats (s. Supported Formats)


Fully redesigned user interface



Import configuration can be saved and reused



Previously imported observed data can be updated from a new data source



Extended data filtering



InVitro-InVivo calibration of intestinal permeability
S. Intestinal Permeability Calibration for details.

New release of the ospsuite R package

Many Fixed issues and Improvements (s. the ospsuite R package news page and the ospsuite R package release page for details)
Significantly improved performance on multicore machines (s. Efficient calculation with the ospsuite R package)
R 4.x is now supported.

S. documentation of the ospsuite R package for detailed description and usage examples.
(Re-)Qualification framework updated
OSP (Re-)Qualification framework is a technical framework to assess the confidence of specific intended use of the OSP platform. This framework allows for an automatic (re)-qualification workflow of the OSP suite. New release of the OSP (Re-)Qualification framework provides some improvements and bugfixes (s. below).

(Re-)Qualification framework is not part of the OSP Suite setup (is only required for the creation of qualification reports) and must be installed separately. The latest release can be found here
Full documentation of the (Re-)Qualification framework can be found here

Fixed issues and Improvements
PK-Sim

Bug comparison chart population
Plots: Default background color does not match
Deleting referenced compound template corrupts the template database
Simulations cannot be loaded from snapshot if used Individual or Population Building block was renamed
Export time profile analysis change the units
Renaming protein in individual does not trigger changed state of simulation
Information in advanced protocol is lost in snapshot
Wrong parameter descriptions
The predefined templates for age groups are not supported anymore
PI: "Id not unique"
Bug: Drug and Metabolite transporter inhibition

MoBi

Tags for event groups
Manual input for event assignment path
Container criteria "in container" only applies for the children of the container

title: Beta-Release of PK-Sim 10 and MoBi 10
body_text:
Beta-Release of PK-Sim 10 and MoBi 10
Dear OSP users,
We are happy to announce that the new Version 10 of PK-Sim and MoBi is now available for beta-testing. The beta-test phase will last until the end of August.
The goal of the beta release is to give you an opportunity to test the new features and to let us know if you have any problems or feedback regarding the new implementation.
How can you test the new features?
You can use the portable versions of PK-Sim and MoBi  (download links below). The portable versions do not require installation and can be used in parallel to the official OSP Suite 9.
Your feedback on the new features is highly welcome.
Important: The beta-test versions are not final releases of the software packages: please use them for testing purposes only, but not for productive work.
Downloads:


PK-Sim 10 beta portable


MoBi 10 beta portable


New features
New population templates (Chronic Kidney Disease) added
3 CKD population templates added (based on the publication Malik PRV, Yeung CHT, Advani U, Dije S, Edginton AN. A Physiological Approach to Pharmacokinetics in Chronic Kidney Disease. J Clin Pharmacol 2020. 60 Suppl 1: S52-S62. doi: 10.1002/jcph.1713):

CKD - Stage 3: defined as patients with GFR of 31 - 60 [mL/min/1.73m²]
CKD - Stage 4: defined as patients with GFR of 16 - 30 [mL/min/1.73m²]
CKD - Stage 5: defined as patients with GFR of    1 - 15 [mL/min/1.73m²]

Each population includes 1000 individuals aged 18-80 years old, 50% male, based on European population (ICRP)

Modeling of Protein expressions in PK-Sim redesigned and extended
See the documentation for details.


More flexible protein localization settings

Enzymes/Binding partners: introduction of fraction expressed in different compartments
Transporters: introduction of fraction expressed apical/basolateral (Kidney/Liver/GI) and fraction expressed blood brain barrier/tissue (brain)


Introduction of Initial Protein Concentration as explicit parameter

See Localizations and initial concentrations of enzymes and Localizations, directions, and initial concentrations of transport proteins for the detailed description.


New transporter types:

Plasma <=> Blood Cells
Plasma <=> Interstitial
Bidirectional



Transport direction (Influx/Efflux/…) can be set in each organ independently



Observed data import in PK-Sim and MoBi redesigned and extended
See the documentation for details.


Better support for "Nonmem like" data formats (s. Supported Formats)


Fully redesigned user interface



Import configuration can be saved and reused



Previously imported observed data can be updated from a new data source



Extended data filtering



Fixed issues and Improvements
PK-Sim

Bug comparison chart population
Plots: Default background color does not match
Deleting referenced compound template corrupts the template database
Simulations cannot be loaded from snapshot if used Individual or Population Building block was renamed
Export time profile analysis change the units
Renaming protein in individual does not trigger changed state of simulation
Information in advanced protocol is lost in snapshot
Wrong parameter descriptions
The predefined templates for age groups are not supported anymore
PI: "Id not unique"

MoBi

Tags for event groups
Manual input for event assignment path
Container criteria "in container" only applies for the children of the container
Molecule keyword replacement does not work for global parameter referencing other global molecule parameters
Drag and drop of parameter to the "references area" does not work

PK-Sim and MoBi

PI Corrupted when referencing a process parameter that is removed from the simulation
Parameter  Identification: When User close and reopen the PI the results section  shown the new boundaries and not those used in the PI run

title: Beta-Release of PK-Sim 11 and MoBi 11
body_text:
Beta-Release of PK-Sim 11 and MoBi 11
Dear OSP users,
We are happy to announce that the new Version 11 of PK-Sim and MoBi is now available for beta-testing. The beta-test phase will last until the end of April.
The goal of the beta release is to give the commmunity an opportunity to test the new features and to report any problems and give feedback regarding the new implementation.
How can you test the new features?
You can use the portable versions of PK-Sim and MoBi  (download links below). The portable versions do not require installation and can be used in parallel to the official OSP Suite 10.
Your feedback on the new features is highly welcome.
Important: The beta-test versions are not final releases of the software packages: please use them for testing purposes only, but not for productive work.
Downloads:


PK-Sim 11 beta portable


MoBi 11 beta portable


New features
[Experimental!] Creating individuals and populations with CKD (Chronic Kidney Disease)
Already in version 10 of the software it was possible to use predefined CKD templates (based on the publication Paul R.V. Malik, Cindy H.T. Yeung, Shams Ismaeil, Urooj Advani, Sebastian Djie, Andrea N. Edginton. A Physiological Approach to Pharmacokinetics in Chronic Kidney Disease. J Clin Pharmacol 2020. 60 Suppl 1: S52-S62. doi: 10.1002/jcph.1713.
With version 11, CKD individuals and populations can now be created dynamically.
When creating an individual, a new option "Disease State" is provided, which allows a selection between Healthy and diseased individuals.



For the details s. the documentation and the original publication by Malik et al.
Expression Profile as standalone building block
Until PK-Sim version 10, expression profiles could only be created within an individual or a population.
Starting with the version 11, expression profiles can be created independently (like all other building blocks) and thus can be reused in different individuals/populations.




For the details s. the documentation
Building block templates can be downloaded directly from the OSP GitHub site



[Experimental!] New preclinical species
Two new preclinical species were added: Cat and Cattle.
The validation of the new species is not fully finalized yet, so both species are provided as an experimental version.
Cattle models can be used with IV applications only.
Extension of Container Criteria in MoBi
Container Criteria can be concatenated now not only by AND but also by OR operator
This extension is partucularly used for the newly introduced Container Criteria of local molecule parameters




Color group observed data when drag and dropping folders to charts
When adding data sets via drag-and-drop of a folder (or multiple folders), all data sets within one folder can be assigned to a single color. This behavior is optional and can be changed by a setting integrated into the context menu of the “Observed Data” entry of the building blocks explorer. This setting is valid for all subfolders.

Alternatively this feature can be toggled on and off from the User Settings:

PK-Sim: under “Utilities” -> “Options” -> “General” -> “Colors”

MoBi:  under “Utilities” -> “Options” -> “Chart Options”


Also, in case the user selects individual observed data sets instead of folders and drags and drops them on the plot, then each one should be assigned a new color, and they should not be grouped according to the folder they belong to.
High Screen Resolution Support
Users reported some issues with the layout of PK-Sim or MoBi when using custom font scaling on 4k monitors (or screen with high resolutions). Some UI elements were hidden, some icons were blurry, some text hard to read etc. The version 11 of PK-Sim and MoBi should address these issues and the tools should now behave as expected, even when using custom font scaling. Give it a try and let us know.
Fixed issues and Improvements
PK-Sim

Add "Urine compartment emptying" as an event template to PK-Sim
Running multiple parameter identifications in parallel
Listing individual and population should order first by type
Active transports Lumen<=> Mucosa: too many transports are created in case the transporter direction (Influx/Efflux/...) is not the same in all GI segments.
Active influx transports Lumen=>Mucosa with the Hill kinetics: cannot create simulations
Load from Template: multiselect+Delete
Symbol Change in Legend for Population Simulation
Clone simulation: Plot title and plot description not copied
Liver zonation: wrong observer formula
Make parameter "Fraction of blood for sampling" visible
AUCR and CmaxR not calculated if formulation with more than 1 Particle bin used
Edit Database Query does not display "old" values correctly
Load project from snapshot: new option "Run simulations"
Display "OK" and "Cancel" buttons in the value origin window

MoBi

Running multiple parameter identifications in parallel
Cannot import molecular weight if it does not match the molecule
Import parameter values from Excel: error if a column without name is present
Import parameter values from Excel: error when a cell contains a formula
Dimension 'Flow per body surface area' not available in DimensionFactory
Deleting a molecule node connected to a reaction does not remove the molecul from reaction
New path keyword: ALL_FLOATING_MOLECULES TODO link

PK-Sim and MoBi

Adding different sheets requires same column names
Importer: MetaData with empty values should not be stored
Importer: Error when measurement column has missing entries
Importer: "Guess" unit each time a column for time or measurement is selected
Importer: setting molweight of imported observed data not possible
Import Observed Data: "Import unit from a column" is case sensitive
Multiselect for color settings in chart editor
Data import tool naming pattern is not working as expected
Importer: loading configuration with molecule not mapped
Importer: "Molecule" MetaData saved to configuration even when not mapped
Data import misaligns values/column header
Data import: group data is not used
Data import cannot handle correctly arithmetic error of a fraction given as percentage
Importer: Handling of "invalid" files
Importer: Cannot select " " as unit for "Fraction"
Importer should perform better when loading several datasets
Importer: "Reset mapping based on current sheet" should be a button.

title: New PK-Sim/MoBi (OSP-Suite) Workshop by esqLABS (and online training portal)
body_text:
esqLABS is conducting a FREE hybrid 1-day workshop on Tuesday June 28th, demonstrating the application of PK-Sim® and MoBi® for use in physiologically-based pharmacokinetics (PBPK) and quantitative systems pharmacology (QSP) modelling and simulation applications. The workshop will be held as an in-person & virtual satellite event to the PAGE Meeting 2022 in Ljubljana, Slovenia.
Participants will learn how to integrate qualified compound model templates to evaluate Drug-Drug-Interactions (DDIs) using PK-Sim how to customize & extend PBPK models to develop PBPK-QSP models (demonstrating upcoming V11 features).
For more details and free registration, please visit our web shop (https://esqlabs.shop/products/workshop) or contact us via email (info@esqlabs.com)
Looking forward to meeting you at PAGE!

title: OSPSuite Version 11 released
body_text:
It is with great pleasure that we are announcing today the release of version 11 of the Open Systems Pharmacology Software Suite.
You can download the version here
As always, do not hesitate to post your feedback or suggestions in the forum.
Release Notes for version 11
New features
Expression Profile as standalone building block
Until PK-Sim version 10, expression profiles could only be created within an individual or a population.
Starting with the version 11, expression profiles can be created independently (like all other building blocks) and thus can be reused in different individuals/populations.

  
  
  

For the details s. the documentation
Building block templates in PK-Sim can be downloaded directly from the OSP GitHub site

  

OSP Platform qualification library and PBPK Models Library
PBPK Models library extended
New PBPK models added:

Ethinylestradiol
Mexiletine
Moclobemide
Omeprazole
S-Mephenytoin
Tizanidine

OSP Qualification reports library extended
New Qualification reports added:

CYP1A2 DDI 
This qualification report evaluates the developed PBPK drug-drug interactions (DDI) models network for the ability to perform simulations with the intended purpose to predict cytochrome P450 1A2 (CYPA12)-mediated DDI.


  


CYP2C19 DDI 
This qualification report evaluates the developed PBPK drug-drug interactions (DDI) models network for the ability to perform simulations with the intended purpose to predict cytochrome P450 2C19 (CYP2C19)-mediated DDI.


  

Model building process and model quality of every new PBPK model is documented in the corresponding model evaluation report.
New releases of OSP Platform qualification library and PBPK Models Library
As with every new OSP Suite release, ALL platform qualification reports and model evaluation reports have been recreated with the new version of the OSP Suite and the latest version of the OSP Qualification Framework:

OSP Qualification Reports library
OSP-PBPK-Model-Library

[Experimental!] Creating individuals and populations with CKD (Chronic Kidney Disease)
Already in version 10 of the software it was possible to use predefined CKD templates (based on the publication Malik PRV, Yeung CHT, Advani U, Dije S, Edginton AN. A Physiological Approach to Pharmacokinetics in Chronic Kidney Disease. J Clin Pharmacol 2020. 60 Suppl 1: S52-S62. doi: 10.1002/jcph.1713).
With version 11, CKD individuals and populations can now be created dynamically.
When creating an individual, a new option "Disease State" is provided, which allows a selection between Healthy and diseased individuals.



For the details s. the documentation and the original publication by Malik et al.
[Experimental!] New preclinical species
Two new preclinical species were added: Cat and Cattle.
The validation of the new species is not fully finalized yet, so both species are provided as an experimental version.
Cattle models can be used with IV applications only.
Extension of Container Criteria in MoBi
Container Criteria can be concatenated now not only by AND but also by OR operator
This extension is particularly used for the newly introduced Container Criteria of local molecule parameters




Color group observed data when drag and dropping folders to charts
When adding data sets via drag-and-drop of a folder (or multiple folders), all data sets within one folder can be assigned to a single color. This behavior is optional and can be changed by a setting integrated into the context menu of the “Observed Data” entry of the building blocks explorer. This setting is valid for all subfolders.

Alternatively, this feature can be toggled on and off from the User Settings:

PK-Sim: under “Utilities” -> “Options” -> “General” -> “Colors”

MoBi:  under “Utilities” -> “Options” -> “Chart Options”


Also, in case the user selects individual observed data sets instead of folders and drags and drops them on the plot, then each one should be assigned a new color, and they should not be grouped according to the folder they belong to.
High Screen Resolution Support
Users reported some issues with the layout of PK-Sim or MoBi when using custom font scaling on 4k monitors (or screen with high resolutions). Some UI elements were hidden, some icons were blurry, some text hard to read etc. The version 11 of PK-Sim and MoBi should address these issues and the tools should now behave as expected, even when using custom font scaling.
New release of the ospsuite R package

Many Fixed issues and Improvements (s. the ospsuite R package news page and the ospsuite R package release page for details)
Major new features:


Adds new DataCombined class that combines observed data (DataSet) and
simulated data (SimulationResults) into a single object. Especially
important is the $toDataFrame() method of this object that returns a data
frame containing combined data from observed and simulated data, which can be
further used for visualizations or other analysis.


Adds new visualization functions:

plotIndividualTimeProfile() and plotPopulationTimeProfile() to create
time-profile plots.



Adds a new class DefaultPlotConfiguration to provide plot configurations for
plotting functions.


Adds simulationResultsToDataFrame() function to convert SimulationResults
objects into a data frame.


All *ToDataFrame() functions also get *ToTibble() variants to extract a
tibble data frame instead of a classical data frame.




S. documentation of the ospsuite R package for detailed description and usage examples.
(Re-)Qualification framework updated
OSP (Re-)Qualification framework is a technical framework to assess the confidence of specific intended use of the OSP platform. This framework allows for an automatic (re)-qualification workflow of the OSP suite.

(Re-)Qualification framework is not part of the OSP Suite setup (is only required for the creation of qualification reports) and must be installed separately. The latest release can be found here
Full documentation of the (Re-)Qualification framework can be found here

Windows 11 support
Windows 11 is now officially supported by the OSP Suite.
Fixed issues and Improvements
PK-Sim

Add "Urine compartment emptying" as an event template to PK-Sim
Running multiple parameter identifications in parallel
Listing individual and population should order first by type
Active transports Lumen<=> Mucosa: too many transports are created in case the transporter direction (Influx/Efflux/...) is not the same in all GI segments.
Active influx transports Lumen=>Mucosa with the Hill kinetics: cannot create simulations
Load from Template: multiselect+Delete
Symbol Change in Legend for Population Simulation
Clone simulation: Plot title and plot description not copied
Liver zonation: wrong observer formula
Make parameter "Fraction of blood for sampling" visible
AUCR and CmaxR not calculated if formulation with more than 1 Particle bin used
Edit Database Query does not display "old" values correctly
Load project from snapshot: new option "Run simulations"
Display "OK" and "Cancel" buttons in the value origin window

MoBi

Running multiple parameter identifications in parallel
Cannot import molecular weight if it does not match the molecule
Import parameter values from Excel: error if a column without name is present
Import parameter values from Excel: error when a cell contains a formula
Dimension 'Flow per body surface area' not available in DimensionFactory
Deleting a molecule node connected to a reaction does not remove the molecule from reaction
New path keyword: ALL_FLOATING_MOLECULES

PK-Sim and MoBi

Adding different sheets requires same column names
Importer: MetaData with empty values should not be stored
Importer: Error when measurement column has missing entries
Importer: "Guess" unit each time a column for time or measurement is selected
Importer: setting molweight of imported observed data not possible
Import Observed Data: "Import unit from a column" is case sensitive
Multiselect for color settings in chart editor
Data import tool naming pattern is not working as expected
Importer: loading configuration with molecule not mapped
Importer: "Molecule" MetaData saved to configuration even when not mapped
Data import misaligns values/column header
Data import: group data is not used
Data import cannot handle correctly arithmetic error of a fraction given as percentage
Importer: Handling of "invalid" files
Importer: Cannot select " " as unit for "Fraction"
Importer should perform better when loading several datasets
Importer: "Reset mapping based on current sheet" should be a button.

Notes

The limitation of max. 5000 output time points per simulation was dropped. Now arbitrary number of output time points per simulation can be defined. This does not have influence on the simulation time but might have impact e.g. on the memory consumption or on the plotting time in charts.
Thus, if you have old projects with very high output resolution - please check it (and adjust if required) before simulating
PDF reports (of simulations and building blocks) are not supported anymore.

title: First hybrid workshop on OSP-Suite of the year! (25 March 2024 @ASCPT)
body_text:
Dear OSP Community,
esqLABS is conducting a 1-day workshop on Monday, 25 March 2024, demonstrating the OSP Suite with PK-Sim® and MoBi® for use in physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) and quantitative systems pharmacology (QSP).  This workshop will be in-person and offer virtual attendance prior to the ASCPT 2024 Annual Meeting.
Participants can expect to learn how to leverage PBPK to evaluate drug-drug interactions (DDI) using PK-Sim® and how to customize and extend PBPK models to develop PBPK-QSP models using MoBi®. The workshop will introduce protein/antibody PBPK modeling for antibody-drug-conjugates (ADCs) for the treatment of tumor growth accounting for target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD).
For more details and how to register, please visit our web shop or contact us via email: training@esqlabs.com.
We’re looking forward to meeting you at the ASCPT 2024 Annual Meeting!
Best wishes,
Nicoleta Spînu
Scientist & Platform Lead Training Business @esqLABS

title: PBPK, PBBM and QSP modelling in Open Systems Pharmacology Suite (24 – 25 June @PAGE2024)
body_text:
Dear OSP Community,
esqLABS is conducting two workshops on Monday and Tuesday, 24 and 25 June 2024, demonstrating the OSP Suite with PK-Sim® and MoBi® for use in physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) and quantitative systems pharmacology (QSP) as well as physiologically based biopharmaceutics modeling (PBBM).  The two workshops will be held in person at the PAGE 2024 Meeting to provide an overview of modeling and simulation applications with OSP Suite.
Workshop 1: The workshops will provide the opportunity to learn how to leverage PBPK to evaluate drug-drug interactions (DDI) using PK-Sim® and how to customize & extend PBPK models to develop PBPK-QSP models using MoBi®. This will be exemplified with a PBPK-based assessment of DDI between Itraconazole and Midazolam and an example of protein/antibody PBPK modeling for antibody-drug-conjugates (ADCs) for the treatment of tumor growth, including kinetics for target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD).
Workshop 2: Participants can expect to gain experience with the in vitro-in vivo correlation (IVIVC) concept and virtual bioequivalence, and apply PBBM to predict dissolution behavior and optimize and assess drug formulations for controlled oral absorption (e.g., explore the dissolution safe space).
For more details and how to register, please visit our webshop:

Workshop 1 (PAGE 2024) – esqLABS GmbH
Workshop 2 (PAGE 2024) – esqLABS GmbH

or contact us via email training@esqlabs.com.
We’re looking forward to meeting you at the PAGE 2024 Meeting!
Best wishes,
Nicoleta Spinu
Scientist & Platform Lead Training Business @esqLABS

title: OSP Community Conference: Pre-Registration and Announcement
body_text:
Dear colleagues,
We are excited to announce the first Open Systems Pharmacology (PK-Sim and Mobi) community conference!
We are organizing a 1.5-day conference, where we will discuss topics related to PBPK modeling in general and, more specifically, our open-source software and its application.
The meeting will be organized in Europe in September/October (likely in Germany or Switzerland).
In the spirit of an open community, we would like to hear from you about what you want to have covered in this event.
Please fill out the form below, where you can also indicate your attendance; we will keep you updated!
Further information on the registration for the event will follow.
 OSP Symposium 2024: Topics & Format
On behalf of the OSP community meeting organization

title: PBBM, IVIVC, VBE In Person Workshop @ACoP (15 November 2024)
body_text:
Dear OSP Community,
We would like to invite you to our second workshop, which will officially take place as part of the program at the ACoP Conference in Arizona.
This workshop aims to present the principles/applications of PBBM with hands-on examples using the OSP Suite, PK-Sim® and MoBi®. The learners will:

Delve into the world of mechanistic oral absorption modeling with PK-Sim®, unlocking a deeper understanding of absorption mechanisms.
Learn how experts are using the OSP suite to forecast dissolution behavior and optimize drug formulations.
Learn about the in vitro-in vivo correlation (IVIVC) concept and its significance in pharmaceutical development and regulatory applications, from streamlining formulation optimization to expediting regulatory approval processes.
Explore ways to establish the IVIVC through the OSP Suite.
Learn about virtual bioequivalence (VBE) and dissolution safe space and how these strategies are reshaping traditional approaches in the drug development space.
Immerse yourself in interactive exercises, gaining experience in VBE analyses with the OSP suite, empowering you to navigate the complexities of pharmaceutical research.

Find more details here.
Please don't hesitate to reach out to us for any further queries! training@esqlabs.com
We’re looking forward to meeting you at the ACoP 2024 Conference!
Best wishes,
Nicoleta Spinu
Scientist & Platform Lead Training Business @esqLABS

body_text:
Dear OSP team, we would like to use R to automatically extract information (Age, Weight, Height, ...) about Individuals. However, in josn only the parameters with updated values are included. For example, if I define an individual with Age=30 and other biometric parameters are set to 'Mean', then in Json only available parameter would be Age.

My questions:

is there a way to force PK-Sim to export the model to json with parameters including the ones with the default values?
when I export individual BB as a markdown file or CSV file, I see all biometric parameters including the default ones. However, export to .md file should be done manually for each individual which is not desired in case of many individuals.

comments.body_text:
is there a way to force PK-Sim to export the model to json with parameters including the ones with the default values?


No. However, you could create a new individual from R with the parameters stored in the json file and then query all other parameters from individual$distributedParameters or individual$derivedParameters (s. https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/create-individual.html)
@Yuri05 I can find the parameter 'GFR' neither in 'distributed' nor 'derived' parameters, any opinion on that?

title: Transit compartment in MoBi
body_text:
Hi all!
I am working on building a model in MoBi for a long-acting intramuscular injectable. The drug profile exhibits a double-peak pattern, indicating an initial fast release followed by a controlled release phase. Do you have any suggestions or best practices for modeling this type of release profile in MoBi? Any guidance on setting up a transit compartment or alternative approaches would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks!

comments.body_text:
Hi, here are my two pence: an alternative approach you could use to approach this empirical is to split the dose in two parts (slow and fast release) and use two Weibull functions to describe the fast and slow release. Similar like Nina Hnke did it for the Rosuvastatin model (https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Rosuvastatin-Model/blob/master/Rosuvastatin-Model.pksim5). Easiest would be to start of in PK-sim with user defined admin protocol like this
.
Than you can modify the advanced protocol in Mobi with the user defined part from PK-sim

and connect a formulation to it:

Hope that might help!
Best, Tobias

Hi Tobias,
Thanks for your reply! I considered splitting the dose in PK-Sim and using the advanced protocol, but it seems like the Weibull function is only available for oral formulations (I am modeling an IM injection). Thanks again!

Just to have others also see a reply we provided offline:

MoBi is flexible enough and it should be reasonably simple to add any type of empirical dissolutions or release function you fancy for your model.
We have outlined this for a double-Weibull on GitHub: Open-Systems-Pharmacology/PK-Sim#1701

Reach out if you need more info to get this implemented (we also hope to have this natively in PK-Sim for an upcoming release (likely V13)).

Hello all,
Thank you for suggesting the double-Weibull function.
Here is the workflow I followed:

In PK-Sim, I created a simple administration protocol using the user-defined administration type, where the entire dose was administered into the muscle interstitial space.
I defined a first-order formulation using the drug half-life.
After running the simulation, I exported it to MoBi, where I modified the event by incorporating the formula of the double Weibull function, with Lag Time 2 affecting the appearance of the second peak.


Here is the simulation results:

The results indicate that whatever is released gets rapidly absorbed, but I don’t believe that reflects reality (especially for the second fraction). I suspect that I may need to introduce a depot compartment, reduce the absorption rate into systemic circulation, or adjust the muscle Kp scaler. It is also worth mentioning that there are no in vitro dissolution data available to describe the release, and the alpha1,2 and beta1,2 values were predicted.
Would you recommend any specific approach to better capture the expected absorption behavior? Are there other parameters I should consider adjusting?
Thanks in advance for your insights!

title: How to calculate Bioavailability with calculatePKanalysis in ospsuite-R
body_text:
Hi all,
I'm using the "calculatePKanalysis" function in ospsuite-R and want to compute absolute bioavailability (F), for oral administered simulations. I know in PK-sim, F is usually calculated by internally running a second simulation with an IV short infusion, using the same parameter for the oral one, then derived from AUCinf.
Is there a direct way to compute the same F using ospsuite-R, if I only have the oral administration simulations in PKML files (outputted from MoBi)?
Any guidance or example code would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks,  Aya

answer.body_text:
There is no easy way to calculate this in R (or MoBi) because the knowledge about "This is IV/Oral simulation" is lost. It is available only in PK-Sim.
The only easy solution I see is to clone the Events building block, manually replace oral administration with IV, create another simulation with the new events building block.
In R, you could then load both simulation, calculate the appropriate AUC and then calculate the bioavailability manually.

comments.body_text:
Hi Aya,
unfortunately, this feature is not supported by the R framework yet.

Unfortunately I do not. I have create a feature request for this (Open-Systems-Pharmacology/OSPSuite-R#1503), but it will take some time until this feature is implemented.
In the meantime, let's hope that someone else has a solution to share.

There is no easy way to calculate this in R (or MoBi) because the knowledge about "This is IV/Oral simulation" is lost. It is available only in PK-Sim.
The only easy solution I see is to clone the Events building block, manually replace oral administration with IV, create another simulation with the new events building block.
In R, you could then load both simulation, calculate the appropriate AUC and then calculate the bioavailability manually.

title: PK-Sim and RNA therapeutics
body_text:
Dear all,
I was wondering whether PK-Sim allows PK modelling of RNA-based therapeutics, and more specifically of antisense oligonucleotides. Are there published examples of such modelling in PK-Sim?
Thank you very much for your help!

comments.body_text:
Hi Christina,
PK-Sim has a large molecule model which has been used for large molecules with a large range of molecular weights (5 -150 kDa). The shape and charge of oligonucleotides might require some model adaptations based on observed data, but this would be a good starting point I think. I am not aware of any specific applications of PK-Sim to oligonucleotides but others might be aware? Feel free to reach out for further discussions!
Best,
Wilbert de Witte

First thing I would try is setting oligonucleotide hydrodynamic radius in the two pore model, adding GFR with fraction 1.0, keeping KdFcRn as very high and then optimizing either specific clearance in the endosome or rate constant for endosomal uptake. This should capture the plasma PK but may not capture the organ specific disposition, most notably the liver. Let me know how it goes.

For antisense oligonucleotides it might be interesting to consider the combination of selecting the “Is small molecule” property at the “Basic physico-chemisty” tab for the compound and using the model for large molecules and proteins. With that, you allow distribution into cells (as with the small molecule model) but you still have the extravasation barrier from the large molecule model. The endosomal uptake & endosomal clearance is switched off in this case and in addition to the renal clearance (glomerular filtration mentioned by Paul) you would most likely need to add an additional plasma clearance. (The general endosomal clearance is probably anyhow a very minor clearance pathway compared to others for these compounds.). Lipophilicity gets then a parameter which is used in principle in the model and for which you probably won’t have a value. Due to the large size of your drug it might have not a large sensitivity however, just try ...

title: Define first-order kinetics for transporters
body_text:
Dear community,
I tried to fit my data in PK-sim by adding drug transporters, using Parameter Identification.
The results suggested that for the some transporters Km are extremely high and Vmax extremely low, which made me think of a 1st-order kinetics, rather than the removal of the tested transporters.
However, I do not find the 1st-order option for drug transporters on PK-sim.

Is there any way to do it?
Any other solutions for the tested transporters?

Thank you so much for your help !

comments.body_text:
Hi @nguyen-tranbach, there is no possibility to add a 1st order kinetic for an active transporter as biology of transporter just dictates saturation.
As a workaround, you could add a new passive transport in MoBi.
Alternatively, you could use Michaelis-Menten type kinetics and set Km to a very high value. If the substrate concentration is much lower than Km, the reaction rate approximates first-order kinetics.

body_text:
Dear all,
How can I get the fraction unbound in gut mucosa (fu,gut) from my model?
Kind regards,
Nina

comments.body_text:
Hello again,
Could somebody tell me please, if the following formula is correct?
Fraction unbound in gut enterocytes = fu,gut = fu,plasma / partition coefficient between intracellular space mucosa and plasma
Best,
Nina
You can get the parameter directly: Organism|SmallIntestine|Mucosa|Duodenum|Intracellular|MOLECULE|Partition coefficient (water/container)

Dear Nina,
I think on a "high level view" your formula is correct. If you wpould like to brake it down to MoBi-speak Pavels path is of correct. To illustrate this a bit further with screenshots from an artificial example you can find the Partitioan coefficiant here in Mobi in the Simulationb Pramateres under e.g. Organism

Or in the Neighbouhoods, where you can see the used formula according to the distribution model used e.g.

Probably interstingly  you get can recalculate the parameter in PK-sim from the simulation-outputs for e.g. Small Intestine-Intracellular and Small Intestine-Intracellular Unbound

Hope you fin this useful.
Best, Tobias

Hi Nina, the FDA reviewer might have been motivated by the fact that fu,gut is often fitted in SimCYP. In PK-Sim, (as long as you didn't change it), this value is predicted based on the partitioning model selected and calculated the same way as for all other intracellular compartments, just using the local tissue composition of the mucosal tissue.

title: Modelling Cattle Data
body_text:
Hello everyone,
I'd like to clear up some basic doubts I'm having with the cattle model in PK-sim.

Besides IV administration, I need to configure the subcutaneous route (SC), which is the most commonly used for administering antiparasitic drugs in this species. However, the latest version (11.3) clarifies that the cattle model should only be used for the IV route.

Can someone explain why?
If I set up the SC route in MOBI, can I use it with any precision?
If so, how should I configure it?


In Parameter Identification, the T1/2 of the formulation varied depending on the dose administered (0.05, 0.2 and 0.5 mg/Kg). In this case, shouldn't the T1/2 be the same because it is a characteristic of the formulation and not of the administration protocol?


Thank you very much for your attention!

comments.body_text:
Dear Bruna,
just some quick thoughts.
I cannot say to much about 1.) as I was not involved in development of the Cattle model so I can only guess here and I think there was only IV data available for validation (see here: #953). Maybe the developers can comment here.
For 2.) It looks like you fitted some dissolution function  to the sc administration. Was it Weibull? / zero or first order? It is a bit hard to say without seeing the data but I think you expected that the t1/2 increases with dose as one would expect a slower release/ diffusion from a larger repository of drug after the injection for the higher doses. That is not the case in your screenshot.
So if you used a e.g. zero order infusion function for your user defined s.c. infusion you might think about using a Weibull were you have more degrees of freedom to describe your data.
B.t.w we are working on a more sophisticated SubQ model and it should be released soon (https://www.page-meeting.org/?abstract=10129)
Best, Tobias

"IV only" might be a bit confusing indeed. What is meant in fact is that there is no oral absorption model for Cattle implemented
(particularly because the 4-compartment stomach was not integrated here).
This should not prevent you from setting up a subcutaneous absorption in MoBi :)

Hi Bruna,
Its not surprising to see different t1/2 in some context of SubCu.. I would suggest to explore the following:

do you have non-linear clearance kinetics?
With subcutaneous administration, drug distribution into systemic circulation can be influenced by local tissue binding and slower diffusion, which may prolong drug presence in peripheral compartments. As dose increases, tissue saturation may alter distribution dynamics, changing the effective half-life. As there is no oral absorption- this may be one place where there would be some answers.

@bruni-cris. I take it that the nonlinearity you show is what the model was fitted to and it matches the data well?
This nonlinearity can be caused by other processes (clearance, binding etc.).
Within the model, there is no reason that your formulation or your absorption from the SC (skin interstitial) compartment should behave nonlinearly related to the dose as long as you don't implement a nonlinear process (not sth. that the default formulation functions in PK-Sim have as a characteristic).
And yes, as @Yuri05 said, the cattle model is just not made for oral, as we have not yet implemented the complex GI tract.
We collaborate with a number of Animal Health companies and this will be added sooner or later as the need is there ;-)

title: Rodgers and Rowland Kp algorithm - Double accounting for protein binding contribution in tissue?
body_text:
Hi there,
I am seeking clarification on the calculation of partition coefficients (Kps) in the interstitial and intracellular spaces in PK-Sim v11.
I created a simulation for a small molecule weak base in PK-Sim and exported it to MoBi to evaluate the calculation for Kp in the interstitial and intracellular spaces (Rodgers and Rowland's Kp algorithm).
I noticed that the equation for Kp (intracellular/plasma) includes binding to plasma proteins present in the interstitial space (i.e., the Ka_PR*T2PR_P_Albumin component):
Kp (intracellular/plasma):

According to the Rodgers and Rowland Kp algorithm (see image below), protein binding (PR) is only accounted for in the interstitial space (i.e., not intracellular space):

Additionally, the equation for Kp (interstitial/plasma) appears to incorporate protein binding (PR) via the component f_protein_int_to_pls * (1/fu - f_water_pls):
Kp (interstitial/plasma):

It seems that the Rodgers and Rowland algorithm (as implement in PK-Sim) accounts for protein binding in both the interstitial and intracellular spaces, which might lead to double-counting. I may be misunderstanding how this is implemented in PK-Sim, so I would appreciate any clarification.
Furthermore, for a strong base molecule under the Rodgers and Rowland Kp algorithm, there should be no need to account for protein binding in the interstitial space. However, the same Kp (interstitial/plasma) equation is used, implying that protein binding in the interstitial space is still included, even though the original algorithm does not account for it.
Can anyone provide clarity on these points?
Thank you!

title: Scaling parameters from adult to pediatrics
body_text:
I have developed an adult PBPK model and scaled it to 6-year-old children.
I can see the demographics in "characteristics of individual" changed to 6-year-old children. However, when I checked renal and metabolic clearances, it was still using 70kg weight and adult blood flow and liver volumes. It seems that the parameters of the
kidney and liver were not scaled. I have attached the snapshots.


Kindly guide if this is normal and ok?

answer.body_text:
@faisalhhu
The scaling of pediatric clearance in PKSim requires input from the user. It is not automatic.
You are correct - the present parameterization of your model with total hepatic clearance and total renal clearance is only appropriate for adults.
The following parameters are updated by default for virtual children in PKSim:

Organ volumes, compositions and blood flows
Glomerular filtration rate
Ontogeny factor for binding to albumin or alpha-acid glycoprotein (your selection)

Clearance is scaled with input from the user. In most cases, scaling clearance to children requires knowing which enzyme is responsible for the metabolism (e.g., CYP3A4), and parameterizing your model with these explicit mechanisms. Then, the specific clearance mediated by CYP3A4 can be scaled by the ontogeny factors which are available in PK-Sim (see: "Ontogeny/Variability like" selection when building your individual).
Scaling renal clearance is usually accomplished by determining the relative contributions between glomerular filtration, tubular secretion and reabsorption (if applicable). The glomerular filtration rate is scaled by default. I would say we do not yet know with certainty how to scale clearance due to tubular secretion, but Kathy Kit-Wun Cheung has some papers on the ontogeny for the relevant transporters, as a start.
I see you are looking for the ontogeny of CES1. I would start with this paper: 10.1124/dmd.116.072652

Finally, I wanted to understand why it is using adult parameters to calculate specific clearance CL_spec and not pediatric calculation parameters?

Because scaling clearance requires input from the user.

comments.body_text: @faisalhhu This is by design. The calculation parameters are used to derive the Specific clearance value. This value is in theory independent from your individual. Only this parameter will be used in your simulation

Thank you for your reply.
The calculation parameters like volume, blood flow rates of the kidney, etc are the same as adult values. Should not we expect some changes in the calculation parameters in pediatrics e.g. a 1-year-old child compared to adults?

In addition, BW is used in the calculation of specific clearance, When we scaled it to a 1-year-old child, the BW of the 1-year-old child should be used in the formula, however, it may be using 73kg in the formula?

@faisalhhu as I said, the only parameter used in the simulation is the CL_spec that is independent of the original individual.
If you look at the formula of your clearance process in your 1year old simulation, you'll see that the value used is 5.32 which was derived from adult parameters.
Thank you. Finally, I wanted to understand why it is using adult parameters to calculate specific clearance  CL_spec and not pediatric calculation parameters?

if I change BW from 73 (Adult BW) to 10 (1 yr old BW) the specific clearance  "CL_spec" changes. From my point of view, I was expecting to see 10kg as BW when calculating  CL_spec for a 1-year-old child instead of 73kg.

Of course, if you change the BW of adult (the reference individual) then the CL_spec will change. The idea is that the CL_spec is calculated based on the experimental values that you have (For instance Plasma Clearance for a 30 years old adult) and then it can be used in other individuals. If you change the base individual, the CL_spec will change

@faisalhhu
The scaling of pediatric clearance in PKSim requires input from the user. It is not automatic.
You are correct - the present parameterization of your model with total hepatic clearance and total renal clearance is only appropriate for adults.
The following parameters are updated by default for virtual children in PKSim:

Organ volumes, compositions and blood flows
Glomerular filtration rate
Ontogeny factor for binding to albumin or alpha-acid glycoprotein (your selection)

Clearance is scaled with input from the user. In most cases, scaling clearance to children requires knowing which enzyme is responsible for the metabolism (e.g., CYP3A4), and parameterizing your model with these explicit mechanisms. Then, the specific clearance mediated by CYP3A4 can be scaled by the ontogeny factors which are available in PK-Sim (see: "Ontogeny/Variability like" selection when building your individual).
Scaling renal clearance is usually accomplished by determining the relative contributions between glomerular filtration, tubular secretion and reabsorption (if applicable). The glomerular filtration rate is scaled by default. I would say we do not yet know with certainty how to scale clearance due to tubular secretion, but Kathy Kit-Wun Cheung has some papers on the ontogeny for the relevant transporters, as a start.
I see you are looking for the ontogeny of CES1. I would start with this paper: 10.1124/dmd.116.072652

Finally, I wanted to understand why it is using adult parameters to calculate specific clearance CL_spec and not pediatric calculation parameters?

Because scaling clearance requires input from the user.

Thank you both for explaining. I shall look more into this. At present, I am modeling the drug and metabolite by using CL info we have in the literature and from the POPPK model we had developed. Also, I was planning to make it a diseased model by changing the blood flow rates, however, I noticed similar flow rates and liver volumes in adults and pediatrics so started to look more at how PKSim is scaling adult values to pediatric values. At present we are assuming the drug and metabolite is eliminated via kidney and there is conversion of the drug to a metabolite (CL of Drug to Met value was added fromPOPPK model) with no biliary CL . I am using renal CL values from POPPK model (Accounted Urine data to calculate renal Cl)  that I think will account for all complex kidney functions.
Thank you all for the explanation.

title: When estimating intrinsic CL with MM kinetics optimized in PK-Sim, is the derived intrinsic CL for "unbound" or "total" drug?
body_text:
Hi everyone,
I was trying to estimate intrinsic clearance with the Michaelis-Menten kinetics that I took from the final model developed in PK-Sim.
For example, the velocity (v) of the active uptake can be expressed as
v = (Vmax * [Substrate]) / (Km + [Substrate])  (Equation 1)
I used the following equation(s) to estimate the intrinsic clearance (CL_int)
CL_int = v / [Substrate] = Vmax / (Km + [Substrate])  (Equation 2)
assuming [Substrate] << Km, Equation 2 can be simplified as
CL_int = Vmax / Km  (Equation 3)
In PK-Sim and MoBi, the active uptake process with Michaelis-Menten kinetics is expressed as
dN/dt = TM * kcat * K_water_src * C_src / (Km + K_water_src * C_src)  (Equation 4)
where TM is the amount of uptake transporter at the source (interstitial space); K_water_src is the Partition coefficient (water/interstitial space); C_src is the substrate concentration in the interstitial space.
Based on Equations 3 and 4, I estimated the intrinsic clearance of active uptake with the corresponding optimized parameters in my developed model using the equation below.
CL_int,actup = (TM * kcat) / Km
However, looking at Equation 4, I am confused about whether the derived CL_int,actup is for "unbound" or "total" drug (Question 1), because I am not sure what K_water_src * C_src represents (Question 2). Does K_water_src * C_src denote the "unbound" concentration in interstitial space?
This is associated with Question 3 - whether CL_int,actup needs correction to become "unbound" intrinsic clearance and how to correct it.
I am aware of discussion #646 where the definition of the Partition Coefficient was explained:

K_water_cell and K_water_int represent the ratio bound to non-water components in the cellular or interstitial space. Their reciprocal value basically represents the unbound fractions in cell and interstitial space (which is why you then often see expressions like [C]/K_water_cell in equations/formulas that define inputs to cellular processes)


Partition coefficients (PC) reflect the ratio "dissolved" within two different "volume fractions" and are dimensionless (so do not convert dimensions). In this case, the PC describes the theoretical concentration ratio of compound dissolved within water and the "fluid" of a specific container (could be "intracellular" with the cellular space fluid consisting of e.g. water, lipids, and protein).
The theory is that only compound dissolved in water (i.e., unbound) is "available" for enzymatic degradation, hence the division of concentrations within that container with the "Partition coefficient (water/container)".

The second explanation makes sense to me, and if I get it correctly,
K_water_int = theoretical concentration dissolved within water of interstitial space/ theoretical concentration dissolved within the fluid of interstitial space
yet it seems to contradict the first explanation -- "the ratio bound to non-water components", which makes me more confused.
Thanks for reading the long description and questions. Any discussion and response is welcome and appreciated.
Kind regards,
Miao-Chan

title: How to adapt size of AxisTicks in R
body_text: I would like to increase the size of the numbers on the x- and y-axis on a plot using the ospsuite-R package. Is this possible using the PlotConfiguration?

comments.body_text:
@nina-nauwelaerts , it is indeed possible by defining DefaultPlotConfiguration objects:
plotConfig <- DefaultPlotConfiguration$new()
plotConfig$xAxisLabelTicksSize <- 16
plotConfig$yAxisLabelTicksSize <- 16

plotIndividualTimeProfile(myDataCombined, plotConfig)
Here is an example taken from the documentation
library(ospsuite)
#> Loading required package: rSharp

# simulated data
simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")
sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)
simResults <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]]
outputPath <- "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"

# observed data
obsData <- lapply(
  c("ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_2.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_3.pkml"),
  function(x) loadDataSetFromPKML(system.file("extdata", x, package = "ospsuite"))
)
names(obsData) <- lapply(obsData, function(x) x$name)

myDataCombined <- DataCombined$new()

myDataCombined$addSimulationResults(
  simulationResults = simResults,
  quantitiesOrPaths = outputPath,
  groups = "Aciclovir PVB"
)

myDataCombined$addDataSets(
  obsData$`Vergin 1995.Iv`,
  groups = "Aciclovir PVB"
)

plotIndividualTimeProfile(myDataCombined)

plotConfig <- DefaultPlotConfiguration$new()
plotConfig$xAxisLabelTicksSize <- 16
plotConfig$yAxisLabelTicksSize <- 16

plotIndividualTimeProfile(myDataCombined, plotConfig)

Created on 2024-12-18 with reprex v2.1.0

You can use the keyword MOLECULE, so Lumen_duo_Duodenum_cell|MOLECULE|Drug absorption rate (lumen to mucosa).

title: Plasma Protein Binding (fu) Definition and Application in MoBi
body_text:
Here's the definition of Fraction Unbound (plasma) in MoBi "Spatial Structure" block:
Min(1 / (1 + (BindingPartner = Albumin ? AlbuminOntgenyFactor : (BindingPartner = Agp ? AgpOntogenyFactor : 1) ) * (1 - fu_ref) / fu_ref) * PlasmaProteinScaleFactor; 1)
How did this equation came from? If BindingPartner isn't defined as neither Albumin and Agp and PlasmaProteinScaleFactor is 1, why the fu is still adjusted from fu_ref?
Dose this Fraction Unbound (plasma) directly applying to the Organism<-VenousBlood<-Plasma<-MOLECULE<-Concentration?
Thank you for your time!

comments.body_text:
Dear @jiangz-98,

How did this equation came from

This equation is used for scaling fraction unbound to populations where albumin concentration is altered and consequently also fraction unbound. It is based on the equation by McNamara & Alcorn, although differently represented. For further details on this equation see the publication by McNamara & Alcorn or by Dallmann et al where the same equation is used.
The Ontogeny Factor is based on changes in albumin concentration in the pediatric population. For albumin, an Ontogeny Factor is implemented in the software but not for Agp ( alpha-1-acid glycoprotein; orosomucoid). Hence, the Ontogeny Factor for Agp is 1, resulting in similar fraction unbound in children. If the binding partner is neither Albumin nor Agp, the Ontogeny Factor is also 1.

If BindingPartner isn't defined as neither Albumin and Agp and PlasmaProteinScaleFactor is 1, why the fu is still adjusted from fu_ref?

fu_ref is the fraction unbound value you manually define in PK-Sim. Thus, if the Ontogeny Factor is 1, the equation above will yield the same value, i.e., fraction unbound = fu_ref.

Dose this Fraction Unbound (plasma) directly applying to the Organism<-VenousBlood<-Plasma<-MOLECULE<-Concentration?

Yes, the Fraction Unbound (plasma) calculated from the equation above will be used in the model, e.g., to convert total Venous Blood Plasma Concentration to Unbound Concentration (this conversion is defined in the Observer Building Block).

title: Beta-Release of PK-Sim and MoBi Version 11 Update 1
body_text:
Beta-Release of PK-Sim and MoBi Version 11 Update 1
Dear OSP users,
We are happy to announce that the new Version 11 Update 1 of PK-Sim and MoBi is now available for beta-testing. The beta-test phase will last until the 15th of December.
The goal of the beta release is to give the commmunity an opportunity to test the new features and to report any problems and give feedback regarding the new implementation.
How can you test the new features?
You can use the portable versions of PK-Sim and MoBi  (download links below). The portable versions do not require installation and can be used in parallel to the official OSP Suite 11.
Your feedback on the new features is highly welcome.
Important: The beta-test versions are not final releases of the software packages: please use them for testing purposes only, but not for productive work.
Downloads:


PK-Sim 11 Update 1 beta portable


MoBi 11 Update 1 beta portable


New features
Mapping observed data sets to simulation outputs in individual simulations
In simulations, the newly introduced Observed Data tab can be used to map observed data sets to the corresponding simulation outputs.
This mapping is required for some of the new features described below (e.g. for displaying goodness of fit, etc.).
All observed data sets belonging  to the opened simulation are listed in the mapping table. When adding or removing an observed data set to/from the simulation, the table is updated. Newly added observed data sets are automatically mapped to simulation outputs according to Organ, Compartment and Molecule meta data of the data set and path elements of the outputs. If no matching output can be found, the mapped simulation output is set to None. This means that the specified observed data set is not mapped. The user can also clear an output mapping by selecting the None entry from the Output dropdown. By clicking the "x" on the right side of the grid, the user can delete the observed data from the simulation.

  

For each mapping, the scaling can be defined as Linear or Log which determines the residual calculation.
Scaling

  
    Linear
    Residuals are calculated as: Simulation value - Observed value. This means that the residuals are defined by absolute differences. If the magnitudes of values are different for different parameters, the different magnitudes of residuals should be harmonized by corresponding weights (reciprocal values).
  
  
    Log
    Residuals are calculated as: log(Simulation value) - log(Observed value) = log (Simulation Value / Observed Value). This means that the ratio of values is considered which is independent of the magnitude of the value.
  

To reflect the quality or importance of the observed data set, you can edit the weights of each mapping.
Predicted vs. Observed Chart in Simulations
For each observed value a point is plotted with observed value as x-Value and corresponding simulated value as y-Value.

  

Adding Deviation Lines to the chart
In a Predicted vs. Observed plot the user can right click on the chart and add deviation lines:

  

This opens a dialog where the user can specify the fold value of the deviation curves.

  

This will create two deviation lines according to the given x-fold value which has to be greater than 1. An x-fold deviation range includes simulated values within x-fold and 1/x-fold of observed values.

  

Residuals vs. Time Chart in Simulations
This chart is similar to the Time Profile chart, but on the y-axis the (absolute) residuals are plotted. The chart includes scaling and weights.
At the top of the chart, the total residual error is displayed.

  

PK-Parameters in Population Simulations
Before version 11 Update 1 pk parameters were only calculated for the selected outputs and were calculated based on the analyzed curve. For example, a mean curve was created and PK Values calculated once from that curve.
With the new release, population simulations contain new features for PK-analyses.
Global PK Analysis
Individual simulations prior to v11 update 1 have had a 'global' PK calculation done using venous blood curve.

  

This type of analysis was not previously available for population simulations. The global pk parameters for population simulation will show in a similar location to the individual simulation, with the caption indicating how the values are aggregated. They are calculated for all individuals in the population and the displayed value is the median of the individual values.

  

These newly available PK-parameters can also be used in the creation of other types of analysis. For example scatter analysis or box plot.

  

PK-parameters for range selections
Now when you select a range analysis for your time profile (e.g. 5%-95%), Pk-parameters will be calculated for the upper and lower range.
Selecting Mean, and Range 5% to 95% as an example:

  

Generates PK-parameters for Mean, 5%, and 95% for each compound.

  

PK-Parameter Aggregation
Also apparent from the screenshot is that there are tabs which display Pk-Parameters aggregated in different ways.
In the tab shown above, 'Aggregated PK Values', PK Values are calculated from the analyzed curve. For example, the curve at the lower limit of the range 5% is used to calculate PK parameters.
Compared to the tab shown below, 'Individual PK Values' where PK Values are calculated for the individuals and then the median is shown.

  

Color grouping tab in the Chart Editor
A new tab called "Curves Color Grouping" has been introduced to the plots with observed data curves. In the tab the user can select one or more metadata, according to which the observed data sets will be grouped and given the same color. Clicking the “Apply Color Grouping” button changes the color of the observed data sets in the chart according to the selection, and then the selection is cleared. The user can then choose a new selection of metadata and change the coloring once again.

  

Editing options of multiple curves
In the “Curves and Axis Options” Tab of the Chart Editor a new context menu item has been added called “Edit options for selected”. It is only visible when the user has selected at least two rows.

  

Selecting this option opens a new dialog that lists the common options of the curves. Initially the values for the options are not set, and if the user lets any of the options to that initial state, that option will not be edited – meaning that the selected curves will retain for that option the value they had before opening the dialog.

  

Grouping of results in the Chart Editor
In the result charts of Simulations and Parameter Identifications (Time Profile, Predicted vs. Observed, Residuals vs. Time) the curves in the Chart Editor are grouped according to their Category. Additionally the checkbox "Link Data to Simulations has been added". When activated, (un)selecting a simulation curve will also unselect the mapped observed data.

  

Running multiple Parameter Identifications in parallel in PK-Sim and MoBi
It is now possible to run multiple Parameter Identifications in parallel. After starting at least one PI, the ribbon bar “Run & Analyze” should reflect the state of the currently selected PI, instead of being equal for all PI instances as it was till now.
Fixed issues and Improvements
PK-Sim

Initial concentration overwritten with a value in Protein Expression BB not propagated into simulation
Snapshot including individual with user-defined initial concentrations for protein expression cannot be reloaded
Expression profile building block shows "0" for all initial concentrations
Curve selection view (Define settings and run) - buttons are hidden in 4k, scaling 200%
UI: Parameter disappears when trying to edit another parameter
UI: Parameter disappears during creation of a simulation
UI: Ontogeny data window - x axis missing in 4K, 200% scaling
Add version to splash screen
UI: Creating population - "Stop" button too small
When overwriting initial protein concentration in Expression Profile: Reset button is active in Individual with no effect
Refresh problem with protein initial concentrations when updating individual from building block
OCT1 Influx expression profile populates on apical side of liver rather than basolateral
PK-Parameter normalized not calculated for statistical curves of a population analysis
Export time profile analysis change the units

MoBi

Wrong icon for events tab
Layout problems in MSV-BB
Mobi 11 returned error while trying to create a new PKSIM molecule.
Inconsistent size of buttons in "Rename" window
UI: Passive transports -> "Add molecule" button is too small
UI: "local reference point" is hidden
UI: formula editor - missing "x" in aliases field
UI: Reaction -> Stoichiometry -> Empty column
UI: unnecessary scroll bars in explorers
Parameter added to an inhibition/induction process should be GLOBAL
Comparison does not include container criteria for parameter
UI: text boxes cut-off in parameter menu
NaNs in boolean formulas

PK-Sim and MoBi

Chart Editor panel automatically resizes and hides plots after every simulation run
Add "g/l" to "Concentration (mass)"
UI: sensitivity analysis - "Update multiple sensitivity parameters" field is not readable.
UI: Parameter Identification feedback
SensitivityAnalysis does not produce any results in certain cases
Calculate AUC_tEnd and C_tEnd for multiple administration simulations
Show a warning message when number of output points is high

Known Issues
PK-Sim

Predicted vs. Observed and Residuals vs. Time plots in a simulation are not saved to snapshot
Predicted vs Observed Plot: it is not possible to plot the data in the different dimensions
Predicted vs. Observed and Residuals vs. Time plots: some graphical settings are lost if the plot was cloned

Notes

Validation of the CKD (Chronic Kidney Disease) population was completed and the "experimental" flag was removed.
The preliminary validation report can be viewed here.

title: Drug concentration in the tissue
body_text:
Dear OSP community,
I have a question regarding a project I am working on. I’m simulating an IV administration of a class II drug in mice. I have observed data for both plasma and liver concentrations, with the liver samples collected fresh and homogenized for quantification.
While I was able to simulate the plasma concentration well (red line and pink dots), the liver profile is a bit off (green line is the liver tissue concentration and blue line is the liver intracellular concentration). The model could not accurately predict the liver Cmax.

I know this drug exhibits lysosomal trapping, and I have accounted for this in the model, but I’m still encountering issues with the liver profile. I have also tried optimizing permeability and partition coefficient without any significant improvement.
So my question is what other parameters or processes may be relevant for the liver profile/simulation?
Any comments would be highly appreciated, and please let me know if anything is unclear.
Thanks in advance,
Luisa

comments.body_text:
Dear Lusia,
overall I would say you simulated liver concentration is not so far of, given that it is often difficult to hit measured data. However, the time course looks quite different. Your model assumed that it is instantly high in the liver following the iv plasma profile. But in you measured data it is peaking after 12 hours. I wonder if you can try to Mimik that behavior by reducing the organ permeability (bring less in the liver) and including an OATP1 uptake transport (to bring it slower in again). Maybe you can play around with that a bit.
Best, tobias

Hi Luisa,
indeed the liver tissue conce looks better now. For po, it looks like you have to fit specific intestinal permeability for mouse. The Charge dependent Schmitt method also reduces calculated intestinal permeability be reducing the mucosa cellular permeability. That might not be realistic and the cellular permeability needs to be reduced in liver only. The alternative would be to keep PK standard for cellular permeability and fit the live cellular permeability only. Than the po data should not be affected so strong. Let me know if this helps
Best, Tobias

title: Renal Impairment population
body_text:
According to the OSPS tutorial and the article by Edgington et al. 2020, I am trying to construct a population with Chronic Kidney Disease. However, the parameters calculated by the equations in the article are not the same as those in the OSPS tutorial attachment. And there is no clear calculation method in the OSPS tutorial.
So how do I set the parameters for the CKD population?

answer.body_text:
PK-Sim provides creation of CKD individuals and populations out of the box now. The implemented algorithm is the one from the paper
Malik, P.R.V., Yeung, C.H.T., Ismaeil, S., Advani, U., Djie, S. and Edginton, A.N. (2020), A Physiological Approach to Pharmacokinetics in Chronic Kidney Disease. The Journal of Clinical Pharmacology, 60: S52-S62. https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.1713
https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-creating-individuals#chronic-kidney-disease-ckd

First, you need to create a CKD Individual



In the next step, you need to create a population based on this CKD Individual

comments.body_text:
The best way is to model this in MoBi:


If you want to stay in PK-Sim (would not recommend it):
You could also "tweak" this in PK-Sim by adding an administration protocol into liver cells in combination with the zero order formulation for the synthesis and first order metabolism in liver cells for the degradation. However it would be pretty error prone and you will need to calculate the "dose", "administration and time" and the "clearance" so that at the end your constants are matched.

title: Easy way to extract user-defined parameters?
body_text:
Dear community,
When reporting PBPK modeling work, we of course like to report on all parameters that were changed from the default value. In PKSim there is a covenient tab within the simulation called 'user-defined' where all these parameters are listed. Is there a straightforward way (e.g. using the OSP-suite-R or esqlabsR package) to extract this list of parameters, including name and value in R?
Thanks for your insights!
Robin

comments.body_text:
Unfortunately, I am not aware of such functionality to extract user-defined parameters from a pkml.
In the Scenario-based workflow in {esqlabsR}, you can get all custom parameter that are applied to the loaded pkml with Scenario$finalCustomParams. An approach would be to always export a simulation with default parameters to pkml and only to change parameters using the Scenarios workflow in esqlabsR. Then you can easily see which parameters were modified in the workflow.

title: Supporting parameters for calculation of pKa- and pH dependent solubilitiy scale factor or logD factor
body_text: I'm looking into the supporting parameters (F1-F3 and K1-K8) for calculation of pKa- and pH- dependent solubilty scale factor or logD factor. Is there any background reading about these parameters and/or could anyone help me understand how they are used in OSP?

comments.body_text:
Unfortunately there is no literature source, I know of, which could explain this. Let me try to explain it in my words. I hope it is not to complicated.
The charging status at a given pH for molecules with multiple chargeable moieties is calculated by calculating the for each possible number of charges its probability. For example, for a molecule containing three chargeable moieties, two acidic and one basic, depending on the pH four different charging stages (i.e. macrospecies) -1, 0, 1 and 2 are possible. Each of the three macrospecies can, however, be composed of different microspecies. E.g. the charge 0 can be achieved by three cases: 1. All moieties are uncharged, 2. The basic moiety and one or the other of the two acidic ones are fully charged. Thus, there are 2^3 = 8 possible microspecies for which the charging status at the given pH has to be calculated and the fractions with which these microspecies occur at the pH is calculated as the product of the probabilities for each moiety to be charged. The latter being determined based on the pKa for each moiety using the Henderson-Hasselbach equation. These eight fractions are the parameters K1 – K8. Finally, for each macrospecies its fraction (= parameters F1 – F3) of occurrence is given by the sum of the fractions for microspecies, by which it can be realized. Please note that for the calculation of logD and solubility it is assumed that each charge, independent of its sign, changes the phys-chem properties by the same factor. For this reason only three “microspecies” are considered instead of four, by not differentiating between +1 and -1.

title: How to implement non-linear protein binding function in PK-Sim/Mobi
body_text:
Hi all,
I am trying to implement a non-linear protein binding function in PK-Sim/Mobi.
The following discussion was found on this topic,
Non linear Protein Binding Function in PK-Sim/Mobi #157

In this discussion, the answer provided was:

Basically, once you have set up your simulation in PKSim you export it to MoBi by "right-click" on the simulation and selecting -> "export to MoBi" in the drop-down menu.
The simulation will appear in MoBi and you can then edit the "fu" directly:

Browse to your Building Blocks and double click on the Molecule Building Block (will have the sam name as your simulation)
On the right, a window will appear with a list of all your molecules in the simulation. Select (double click) the one for which you ant to change the fu.
Again, further to the right you will see two tabs with one named "Parameters". Select this one and browse to the parameter "Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)".
There under "Value" change (with drop-down menu) from "Constant" to "Formula"
Enter the formula you want to use to describe your observed nonlinearity (please refer to the help menu ( the question mark on the very top right of the MoBi window) on how to create a formula
recreate your simulation --> run (also here the help menu may help)


However, I can't figure out the correct formula to use. This is what I am trying to implement, with Concentration in molar.
Concentration< 0.001 ? 0.10 : 0.10 + 5.4*Concentration
where Concentration was defined as an Alias with Dimension Concentration (molar), however, I can't seem to figure out which Path to use for the Concentration Alias.
The error is always that this alias references an entity with a path that cannot be found. I also could not find it in the manual/help menu.
Thanks in advance for any response

comments.body_text:
Dear Michiel,
thanks for your inquiry.
"fu" is a global parameter/property of a molecule, while "concentration" is "local", thus depending on the location of the molecule. You will not be able to use a local reference path definition (relative path) for the "concentration" in the formula you set up but would have to decide of a "representative" concentration value, here. So this could e.g. be either the arterial or venous blood plasma concentrations (use the full absolute path definition (global reference point)).
For your formula, please consider that fu can never be > 1, so I would use a saturatable function (Michaelis Menten or Hill equation) or a capped function, i.e., including "concentration > 1" condition:
'Concentration < 0.001 ? 0.10 : (Concentration > 1? 1 : 0.10 + 5.4*Concentration)'
Best,
Stephan

Thank you Stephan for your response. I got it working now by your help pointing me in the right direction.
The path to use that seems to work is:
Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|MOLECULE|Concentration
The main issue was that the easy drag-and-drop menu of possible references objects was not visible and I could not scroll down further.

Versus how it should look like after removing the notification view and widening the windows:

Without this menu it was very difficult to think of the right syntax to use in the path...
Thanks.
p.s. I fully agree with the issue on fu > 1, it was just a random formula that I typed
