Version 1.5-OSP11.2 of the qualification report on the simulation of CYP3A4-mediated drug-drug interactions has been released.
The full report is available here.
Major updates

Carbamazepine has been added to the CYP3A4 DDI network: The interactions between carbamazepine as perpetrator and the victim drugs alprazolam, efavirenz, and midazolam are included, as well as the interactions between carbamazepine as victim and cimetidine, efavirenz, and erythromycin as perpetrators.

Minor updates

title: PBPK-PD/QSP Workshop opportunities during ACoP
body_text:
Dear OSP-Community,
If you are visiting ACoP this year, don't miss to take the opportunity to join our PBPK-workshop:
November 8-9, Workshop: PBPK-PD/QSP modeling with the OSPSuite (PK-Sim®, MoBi®) with Tobias Kanacher, Ph.D., Senior Consultant, Gianluca Selvaggio, Ph.D.,Consultant.
This workshop features PBPK and PD/QSP modeling with the Open Systems Pharmacology (OSP) suite, also known as PK-Sim® and MoBi®. The focus is on how to leverage the open source platforms PK-Sim® and MoBi® to utilize the standardized, and well established, whole body PBPK model provided in PK-Sim®, combining it with the open modeling possibilities in MoBi® to finally creating WB-PBPK-PD/QSP models.

Tobias and Gianluca

title: How to predict concentrations in ELF（Epithelial Lining Fluid） using MOBI？
body_text:
Hello, everyone，
I want to predict the drug concentration in the ELF(Epithelial Lining Fluid) of lung after an antibiotic is given by injection.
According to my previous understanding, PK-Sim could not achieve this function. I tried it in MoBi by referring to the published literature (#927 ), but I'm not sure if the structural  and the transporter formula are set up correctly.
I mainly carried out the following steps:


ELF subcompartment was created（ spatial strucues -tree-organism-lung path）. The ELF related physiological volume and area parameters were input.



Link the blood circulation between ELF subcompartment and cells（ spatial structrus-diagram path）.



The permeability parameters of ELF subcompartment were created （molecules-parameters path）



Finally, and most importantly, I created a passive transports （ passive transportspath） to characterize drug transport between cells and ELF.




I don't know if these steps are correct, especially the last formula. I hope you can help me. Thank you in advance

title: Large Molecule PK curve shape
body_text:
I'm trying to make a brain model for large molecules (mAb), but fitting the plasma curve is a bit hard.
Currently, I have some observed data of a molecule and I am trying to adjust the Kd (FcRn) in endosomal space of the molecule to fit the plasma curve. But as seen in the picture below, I am not sure how to get the curve shape (red line) to be steeper at the beginning then plateau.

From what I know, metabolism of large molecules (mAbs) in PK-Sim is mainly dictated by the endosomal clearance, and FcRn recycling also plays a role in this. The rate of specific clearance is determined by (rate of uptake - rate of recycling). I can lower the rate of recycling to steepen the curve and fit the observed data better, but that wouldn't really make sense physiologically to have different rates of recycling for different molecules, or would that be okay? Are there any other mechanism/parameters that I am missing here regarding large molecules?
Below is an example of fitting the plasma curve (Red line at top) by changing Kd and rate of recycling at quite extreme values (Kd = 0.02µmol/L, recycling = 0.0002477 1/min)

This works well for this specific molecule, but for another, the curve just does not fit when keeping the rate of recycling constant. Are there any other parameters that would make more sense to change per molecule other than Kd? Ultimately, I am trying to see if I can predict CSF conc. by using plasma conc. data and fitting the curve to just the plasma conc. via Kd, but maybe I need to fit more than just the Kd parameter.

comments.body_text: What about target binding of the antibody? This might explain the shape...

What species is this? Model is challenging to use in Rats at this time.
Curve shape looks unusual to me - are you using the recommended 5.34 nm hydrodynamic radius for a mAb? Is the FcRn binding affinity (Kd) in the model within a standard range (i.e., somewhere between 100 nM and 1.5 uM for most mAbs?).
Paul

title: Hybrid workshop with examples in PK-Sim® and MoBi® (4-5 November 2023)
body_text:
Dear OSP Community,
We are delighted to share the news of an upcoming hybrid workshop that we are happy to invite you to.
This two-day event is packed with lectures and practical assignments on how to use PK-Sim® and MoBi® with small and large molecules. Included are topics such as drug-drug interaction (DDI), age-dependent PK in pediatrics, and protein/antibody PBPK modeling for antibody-drug-conjugates (ADCs) for the treatment of tumor growth accounting for target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD).
The workshop will be given by our CEO and Lead Scientist, Dr. Stephan Schaller, together with our Principal Scientist, Dr. Wilbert De Witte, as a pre-meeting workshop before the upcoming ACoP14 conference.
We offer free places for online attendance and have a limited number of free and discounted places for the in-person event.
Book your ticket at Workshop (ACoP14 2023) – esqLABS GmbH.
Please reach out to us for any further queries! training@esqlabs.com.
Best wishes,
Nicoleta
Scientist & Training Platform Lead @esqLABS

title: Defining the "Dimension" of a parameter in Mobi
body_text:
Dear all，
I want to define a "dimension" in mobi, the unit should be "Inversed mass (/g)''. It seems that there is no such "dimension". I also tried to use "dimensionless" to represent it, but it doesn't seem to fit my purpose.
What should I do?

answer.body_text:
You can extend the MoBi dimension factory ("C:\ProgramData\Open Systems Pharmacology\MoBi\11.2\OSPSuite.Dimensions.xml") with the definition of new Inversed mass dimension and restart MoBi
    <Dimension name="Inversed mass" baseUnit="1/kg" defaultUnit="1/g">
      <BaseRepresentation lengthExponent="0" massExponent="-1" timeExponent="0" electricCurrentExponent="0" temperatureExponent="0" amountExponent="0" luminousIntensityExponent="0" />
      <Units>
        <Unit name="1/kg" factor="1" />
        <Unit name="1/g" factor="1E3" />
        <Unit name="1/mg" factor="1E6" />
        <Unit name="1/µg" factor="1E9" />
        <Unit name="1/ng" factor="1E12" />
        <Unit name="1/pg" factor="1E15" />
      </Units>
    </Dimension>

title: A PB-QSP Model of the incretin hormones GLP-1 and GIP and a DPP4-inhibitor sitagliptin
body_text:
Dear all,
we are happy to share a PB-QSP model of secretion and elimination of the incretin hormones GLP-1 and GIP and the DPP4-inhibitor sitagliptin. The model has been developed in MoBi and can be integrated into the published QSP Glucose-Insulin-Model.
The model can be downloaded from its repository, the respective publication is
Balazki, P., Schaller, S., Eissing, T. & Lehr, T. A Physiologically‐Based Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Model of the Incretin Hormones GLP‐1 and GIP and the DPP4 Inhibitor Sitagliptin. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst. Pharmacol. psp4.12520 (2020).doi:10.1002/psp4.12520

comments.body_text:
Dear Mr Balazki,
(What a great study!)
I am a modeling noob and there are a few things I don't understand while studying this article that I would like to ask you about.


When describing the degradation process of incretin hormones, I found in the mobi file that there is a parameter "K_water" included in the formula, but there is no mention of this parameter in the supplementary file. What is the role of this parameter here?




Why is the metabolism of GLP-1 (7-36)amide and (9-36)amide to unknown metabolites by NEP a linear process, whereas the degradation of GIP (1-42) to unknown metabolites by NEP is modeled as a saturation process? What is the basis for defining the type of degradation process here? (and other descriptions of degradation processes)


For incretin hormones, the large molecule module is used for modeling, while for sitagliptin, the small molecule module should be used. How to merge small molecule and large molecule in mobi's "Molecules" module? Should I use "Merge" or just select both in PK-sim? (If the latter, should I use the large molecule module or the small molecule module in the simulation?)


The R package "MoBiToolboxForR_8.0.15" for graphing cannot be installed in R4.0.0 or above, is there any alternative?


With best regards，
SCZ

Hi @SCZ1174 ,
sorry for the delayed answer.


k_water is the partitioning coefficient of the molecule in the specific container, so basically the free fraction of the hormones in the interstitial space. As we assume the fraction unbound in plasma as 100% for the hormones, the value of k_water is also 1.


DPP4-mediated degradation processes are modeled as saturable reactions as prior information about the Km value exists (in vitro measured). For processes that we do not have any prior information, as in case of NEP mediated degradation (we are using NET as a surrogate for the "residual" degradation and do not know exactly what this process is), we usually assume linear kinetics and only add saturability if the data suggests it. In case of GIP (1-42), it was necessary to make the process saturable and fit the Km value in order to describe the data.


The "basic" PBPK model was created in PK-Sim and combined all molecules that are used in the final model. For this, a large molecules model was selected in PK-Sim, which allows simulation of both large as well as small molecules.


Unfortunately, the support of this R package has been discontinued and there is no version of R4 available.

You can select the "Model for large molecules" and combine macromolecules (with the box "Is small molecule" unchecked) with small molecules (with the box "Is small molecule" checked) in one simulation.

title: Published: Open Systems Pharmacology Community-An Open Access, Open Source, Open Science Approach to Modeling and Simulation in Pharmaceutical Sciences.
body_text:
Perspective: Open Systems Pharmacology Community-An Open Access, Open Source, Open Science Approach to Modeling and Simulation in Pharmaceutical Sciences.
Open-Systems-Pharmacology/OSP-based-publications-and-content#216
Systems pharmacology integrates structural biological and pharmacological knowledge and experimental data, enabling dissection of organism and drug properties and providing excellent predictivity. The development of systems pharmacology models is a significant task requiring massive amounts of background information beyond individual trial data. The qualification of models needs repetitive demonstration of successful predictions. Open Systems Pharmacology is a community that develops, qualifies, and shares professional open source software tools and models in a collaborative open‐science way.

title: Error from parameter identification in PK-Sim
body_text:
Hi,
I developed a model with PK-Sim first, then with MoBI. I then imported again the model on PK-Sim and tried to do parameter estimations there. The simulation runs normally, and never gave any error message, but if I try to perform a PI in PK-Sim I get the following error message.

Could anyone point me in the direction of the origin of this error message? I tried to change the values of the parameters and algorithms, but the error remains. In MoBi the parameter estimation with the same simulation runs normally.
Thanks a lot for your advice!
Donato

comments.body_text:
Hi Donato, so it seems the model is not stable, i.e., the solver cannot solve it.
But, the re-import of MoBi Models to PK-Sim is (only) intended for population simulation.
Nevertheless, it might be due to different solver settings in PK-Sim vs MoBi, did you check if they are the same?

Hi Stephan,
Thanks for your reply. Actually, you can also import individual simulation in PK-Sim from MoBi, not only for population simulations, and this is what I did.

I find simulations easier to handles in PK-Sim, for summary plots and PK analysis.
Anyway, I checked the settings of the parameter identification, and they are identical between MoBi and PK-Sim.
I also checked the solver setting and they are the same as well.
Any other idea?
Thanks,
Donato

@teutonicod Can you increase the simulation resolution (e.g. set it to 100 points/minute) in the first 3 minutes?
What's the error message then in the PI?

The PI fails with the above message if it tests a parameter set that cannot be solved.
Try using the "Monte Carlo" algorithm, it will not fail in this case but will try another parameter values set instead.

title: What are the factors that affect the proportion of feces excreted?
body_text:
Hello everyone,
I'm building a model by fitting different clearance pathway ratios and I'm having a little problem.
The fecal excretion ratio of my drug is 13%. I used the following method to try to fit it, but I don’t know if it is suitable:

Add bile clearance. (I saw an increase in bile clearance ratio, but no change in fecal clearance ratio.)
Add the enterohepatic circulation process on the basis of 1. (Added "Galbladder emptying enabled" to the event module and set "EHC continuous fraction" to 0.5. It was found that the bile clearance ratio decreased, but the feces clearance ratio remained.)
Delete the enterohepatic circulation process, assume an efflux secretion protein and set its expression in each intestinal segment, and the fecal clearance ratio changes!


Why does the proportion of feces excretion remain unchanged after bile is added for elimination?
After adding the enterohepatic circulation process, the reason why the fecal excretion ratio remains unchanged is that the drug is absorbed again? And why does the bile clearance ratio decrease?
Do I have to add secreted proteins from the gut if I want to fit the fecal excretion ratio?

comments.body_text:
To achieve fecal excretion, you have to limit intestinal absorption of the compound in some way. This can be done either by limiting the solubility or the intestinal permeability of the compound. Only if the absorption is limited in some way, you will get an increase of fraction excreted to feces after introducing biliary clearance.
Limiting intestinal permeability can be done by directly reducing the parameter "Specific intestinal permeability" or introducing efflux transporters like P-gp.

title: Lung blood flow rates
body_text:
Hi everyone!
I want change the blood flow rates of lung in my model but I cannot modify this parameters. Would anyone tell me how do?
I will appreciated!
Thankyou!

comments.body_text:
Hi,
actually the blood flow rate in lung is the sum of the organ specific blood flows of the organs which are supplied with fresh blood from the lungs. You can see the formula and the organs by mouse over the lung blood flow.

So if you increase e.g. the bone blood flow, this is automatically reflected in the lung blood flow. Maybe the best way would be to increase all involved organs by the same factor to get the lung blood flow you want.
Hope that might help you.
Best, Tobias

title: Possible Biases When Applying the Rodgers & Rowland Model
body_text:
Hi, all
In MOBI, I find that the mass transfer from the interstitium to intracellular space using Rodgers & Rowland Model is defined as K_water_int * PA_int_cell * C_int - K_water_cell * PA_cell_int * C_cell, wherein the K_water_cell is actually the reciprocal of the tissue to plasma free drug partition coefficient (Kpu) given by  Rodgers & Rowland.
In the definition of Kpu, the organ is taken as a whole, and therefore the distribution into both extracellular and intracellular components is considered and lumped into the Kpu.
However, in PK-Sim, where the extracellular and intracellular spaces are separated, the intracellular concentration C_cell is modified by the partition coefficient K_water_cell which still includes the distribution into the extracellular component. Would this lead to potential biases in simulation?

comments.body_text:
We are aware of this issue and are working on an improvement.
The change may affect the simulation results depending on the properties of the used compound etc.

title: Liver volume
body_text:
Hi! I plan to use physiological parameter values from PKSim in my calculations and I have a question about liver compartment volume in a typical human - do I understand correctly that it's 2.38 L? It's a bit bigger than I expected and I followed the link and it's about 1.5 L. May I ask if I correctly understand the attached table?
Thank you in advance!

answer.body_text:
Hi @VeronikaVor2, PK-Sim assumes a density of 1 g7cm³ for all organs, hence kg and L are equivalent. The following two scaling steps are needed to convert the value from the ICRP report to those in PK-Sim:
The ICRP Report gives a typical liver weight of 1.8 kg for a 30 years-old male individual (Table 2.8, p. 18). However, this weight refers to the port-mortem liver drained of blood. To get the the in vivo liver weight including blood, you need to account for the fraction vascular which is 0.17 for the liver:

So the in vivo liver weight including blood is 1.8/(1-0.17) = 2.17 kg
The second step includes an up-scaling of all organs in PK-Sim to obtain the final body weight. The background is that PK-Sim does not consider all organs of the human body (e.g., thyroid, thymus, eyes, and several glands are missing), so the sum of the in vivo weights of organs implemented in PK-Sim is approx. 9 % lower than the body weight. To match the total body weight, all organs are thus scaled again by a factor of 1.09 which finally gives you the PK-Sim value:
1.8/(1-0.17) * 1.09 = 2.36 L, which is the liver volume for the 50th percentile in PK-Sim.

title: How to build PBPK/PD models?
body_text:
Hi,
I am working on a antidiabetic drug. Now I have it's plasma concentration-time profile and changes in FPG and HbA1c. Is there a way that I could use the PBPK model I built in PK-Sim and transported it to MoBi to build a PBPK/PD model? Is there any tutorial? What PD model is the best fit? Thanks very much for your help!

answer.body_text:
You can check out one of our PBPK-PD models on glucose homeostasis and the corresponding publications:

https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Glucose-Insulin-Model
https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/SGLT2i-hyperfiltration-model
https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Incretins-Model

comments.body_text:
You can check out one of our PBPK-PD models on glucose homeostasis and the corresponding publications:

https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Glucose-Insulin-Model
https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/SGLT2i-hyperfiltration-model
https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Incretins-Model

At esqLABS, we have a few self-paced tutorials available that could help.
These ones would be in particular relevant for learning about PK-Sim and MoBi interaction:

https://esqlabs.shop/products/a-two-container-model-2
https://esqlabs.shop/products/observer-2
https://esqlabs.shop/products/formula-types-2

These ones are applied case studies, unfortunately, not in diabetes, which could also help how to use both PK-Sim and MoBi:

https://esqlabs.shop/products/dermal-absorption-of-caffeine-2
https://esqlabs.shop/products/maternal-fetal-pbpk-2
https://esqlabs.shop/products/antibody-therapy-in-oncology-2

If you are working in academia, consider getting free access at this link https://learn-esqlabs.talentlms.com/
If you have any questions, feel free to reach out at training@esqlabs.com

title: Problems in Plasma Clearance
body_text:
Hi, all
When I learned the equations in Hepatic clearance via the Liver Plasma Clearance, I managed the equations involved as follows:

The first two equations are the calculation of the intrinsic clearance and the specific clearance (omitting the body weight). The following equations are extracted from MoBi.
After combining these, I think the metabolism rate would be (Intrinsic_clearance * Water_to_intracellular_partition * Intracellular_concentration)/Liver_cell_fraction, which is not equivalent to Intrinsic_clearance * Plasma_unbound_concentration or Plasma_clearance * Plasma_concentration. I think the Liver_cell_fraction is redundant.
Did I make any mistake in the derivation?
I also have some questions about why the equation of specific clearance in Liver Plasma Clearance is different from that in Kidney Plasma Clearance. The equation for the kidney is:

I find that the above formula can be derived based on the assumption that only the change in the plasma drug concentration contributes to the clearance:

However, as the drug in plasma is eliminated, the drug originally present in the red blood cells will also be distributed into the plasma, supplementing the drug loss. Therefore, might this equation underestimate the elimination?

title: How to model extended-release drug?
body_text:
Hi!
I am trying to build a drug with extended-release formulation which consists of two different release stages. How can I set up this formulation in PK-Sim or MoBi? Is there any tutorial or publications to follow?
Looking forward for your reply!
Best regards,
Greta

comments.body_text:
Hi! I am trying to model this process in MoBi.
This drug has an initial quick release in the first few hours, and then released slowly in the next few days.
Here is what I am doing:

I am trying to change Dose in Events Tab to a  function related to time as seen in the picture. Is that right? Cause it's a little difficult for me to figure out the formula and input it in MoBi. Can you give me some advice? Or is there any tutorial or MoBi file that I can follow?
Really looking forward for your help!
Best,

Dear Greta,
just some quick thoughts: maybe it is easier to start in PK-Sim. There you can create and advanced dosing protocol with one Dose event for the quick release and one for the slow release part of your formulation. A bit similar as Hanke et al described it for the Rosuvastatin model ( 10.1007/s11095-021-03109-6). You can combine the fast and slow release process with respective formulations. Formulations and Administration protocols are easier to set up in PK-Sim. Later you can modify the Events BB in Mobi, too.
Hope this might help you.
Best, Tobias
You could also approximate your ER formulation in Excel (selecting an appropriate time raster), then create a table formulation in PK-Sim and import your formulation from Excel.

title: PK-sim for antibodies?
body_text:
Hello!
I want to use PK-Sim to get the PK/PD and dosing concentrations for antibodies being developed.
What all input parameters are needed for using PK-sim for antibodies specifically? This is my first time using PK-Sim, so if anybody has successfully used it for the same purpose, please let me know. Thanks!

comments.body_text:
Hi Ritu @rituran ,
certainly, we have extensive expertise in utilizing PK-Sim for biologics PK/PD assessments.
Most crucial will be your molecular weight, FcRn binding affinity in endosomes, and possibly target binding kinetics (Kd, koff) and kinetics of the target itself (expression levels, turnover rates), should it be relevant for target-mediated clearance.
Best,
Stephan

Hi Ritu,
just as an addendum to Stephan's answer above: For target mediated clearance, there is usually also a degradation rate constant for the antibody-target-complex. The processes of target turnover and degradation of the antibody-target-complex are added in MoBi.
For the "base" model in PK-Sim there is also the solute radius, which is calculated from the molecular weight by default. You can also enter directly an experimental value for this. 5.34 nm were used for antibodies during the development of the large molecules model in PK-Sim.

title: How do I construct a population with liver damage in PK-Sim?
body_text: I first modified the corresponding physiological parameters in the individual, and then in the population, I scaled the corresponding individual to the population. However, according to the final simulation results, there is a large deviation between the population and the individual simulation values. How do I scale an individual with modified physiological parameters to a population in PK-Sim?

comments.body_text:
Hi, generally the differences see in Population simulations and and individual simulation can have several reasons. Hrad to comment without seeing the project . But just some quick thoughts, to scale an individual you cane scale it with the inbuild scale function:

However to investigate the differences between your population simulation and your individual simulation a bit deeper it might be a good idea to extract an typical (our an extreme) Individual from your created population

you can chose the individual in the next step by giving the individual ID.
Than build agan a individual simulation with the extracted individual and compare the original individual Simulation with the mean individual and see what differences are there.
Hope that might help you.
Best, Tobias

title: The concentration of intravenous bolus
body_text:
Hi everyone.
I have simulate a drug with intravenous bolus administarion,but I wonder why the concentraion of the first administation is higher than next. I will very appreciate if anyone could tell me the reason!

comments.body_text:
Hi @Supercn01,
I is rather challenging to say without having the simulation file nor knowing your model configuration and parameter.
Possibility the resolution (time steps) of your simulation are different over the time profile.
The default is that the first hour is rather fine grained and the later time frames rather rough.
Try using the same simulation resolution for the whole time frame.
Best
Henrik

title: Accessing Github repositories with TortoiseSVN
body_text:
Hi all,
Since some time I wanted to use TortoiseSVN with a repository hosted on GitHub, and I was unable to access the repository when the repository was asking for an identification. I recently found the solution and wanted to share it, in case it could be of any help to others. An identification with GitHub credentials would be asked if the repository is private or on a public repository for any commit.
When identification is requested by TortoiseSVN, it should be provided the userID for GitHub, but the password should be replaced by a personal access token (PAT). A PAT can be created following these steps
https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/creating-a-personal-access-token
When creating the PAT, you will need to provide the scope of the token, which would be related to the permissions of the token itself.
For working on a public repository, you can select the option below public_repo

For a private repository, you can select the full repository option

Please notice that PAT must be saved somewhere directly after creation. Once the user closes the PAT-creation form on GitHub, the PAT cannot be shown ever again. So if you don’t write it down (or copy-paste directly), you will need to create another one.
This identification is compatible and independent from 2-factor authentication.
Thanks a lot to @Yuri05 for clarifying some of these points!
Hope this is helpful!
Donato

comments.body_text: Thanks for sharing, @teutonicod !
thanks a lot... you save my time... thanks again :)

title: How to establish an indirect pharmacodynamic protein in Mobi and observe it?
body_text:

If a protein is an indirect pharmacodynamic site, not directly binding to the drug but influenced by it, should this protein be initially created in the individual within PK-Sim and then transferred to Mobi? Or should it be directly established in Mobi?
If the protein is present in secretions, how should I define it and observe it?
Are there any literature or manuals mentioning this?
If anybody has successfully used it for the same purpose, please let me know.
Thanks very much for any reply.

comments.body_text:
Is it a circulating protein? It depends on if you require the protein to be distributed on a whole-body level or just locally.
If whole-body, criculating, and if it gets secreted, implement it in PKSim and add secretion and any dynamics in MoBi.

thank  you very much @StephanSchaller！
In fact, there are two types of indirect pharmacodynamic proteins that I need to observe: one circulates throughout the body, and the other is locally secreted.
I am currently focusing on the pharmacodynamic proteins that are locally secreted, which are protein complexes excreted in feces.
I have already connected them in "Individual" in PK-Sim and transferred them to Mobi. I added the production and metabolism components in the reaction, and included them in the "concentration in feces" section of the molecule observer.
However, I cannot find them in "define settings" and can only see my drugs. Am I doing it right?


However, there is still an issue bothering me.
This secreted protein is commonly measured in feces using the unit mg/kg (ug/g). Even if I can observe it in feces, I am unable to capture its correct dimensional representation. This is because feces in the spatial structure only has volume.
Could you provide some advice on addressing this concern? I would greatly appreciate it

title: Changing gastric pH doesn't affect the PK profile of weak base drugs with pH-dependent solubility
body_text:
Dear All,
I am working on a weak base drug with pH-dependent solubility. It did not show any change with the change of gastric pH. But I noticed that the solubility decreased indeed with the increase of gastric pH in advanced parameter page of the simulation. Dose the setting of formulation or pKa value affect the result? I wonder why the change of gastric pH does not connect to the PK profiles.
Besides, how can I obtain the fa value in PK-sim?
Thanks a lot.

comments.body_text:
Dear @wuershaonian,
Formulation settings do not influence the solubility, but pKa values obviously do so. They are used to calculate the solubility at a given pH value according to the Henderson Hasselbach equation. You can visualize the solubility-pH profile of your drug in the Compound Building Block here:

The solubility along the GI tract will be the same as displayed for the relevant pH values in this graph. You can find more information in the Manual here under the section solubility.
To get the fa, you need to select it as output under Define Settings and Run --> Lumen:

If you have more than 1 output in simulations: did you assign it in the comparison?

Did you calculate the simulation outputs in the population simulations which you are trying to compare? If you have more than 1 output in simulations: did you assign it in the comparison?

@Yuri05 Is it because I'm using two different drugs?

Is it because I'm using two different drugs?

Yes, this might be a reason. Comparison will only allow choosing an output which is the same between simulations.
"Same output" means here: same location, same drug.
If there are no interactions between those 2 drugs: you could create a new simulation with both drugs applied and compare the outputs directly in this simulation.

title: Unusual concentration versus time profile in lung tissue
body_text:
Hello,
I recently developed a PBPK model using PK Sim for a drug with a Log P of 2. My goal was to predict the concentration of the drug in lung tissue after oral administration of this drug. However, when I ran the simulations to predict the lung tissue concentrations using the default PK Sim simulations mode, I noticed that the concentration versus time profile for the lung tissue concentration (black line in the attached figure) showed an unusual pattern with no decline in concentrations. In my opinion, the simulations should show that the levels in the lung must decline. Could anyone please comment on this issue and share if they had similar experiences.
The red line is the simulated plasma concentration and the dotted line is the observed plasma concentration from a clinical study.

answer.body_text:
Hi Kash,
I could not find clearance with enzymes in the simulation. The Expression of enzymes was not implemented in the model:

I then added a total hepatic CL:

and reduced lipophilicity from 3.3 to 3 (I thought it should be 2?):

Not sure this represents the truth, but it allows matching the lung data.
Please also check ou training courses (free for academia) for all sorts of applications of PK-Sim and MoBi:
https://learn-esqlabs.talentlms.com/
Maybe there is something that can help you as well.
Best, Stephan

These clearance processes were implemented in the ADME section of the compound file. The reported half-life of the drug is ~19 hours. Do we need to implement additional clearance processes elsewhere in the model to account for the clearance in lung tissue?

--> Actually this question should be asked in the forum ;-)
But it looks like your clearance is not active (plasma is not declining either)?
If you can share your project you can attach it here as zipped file.

title: Intranasal Model to brain Using MoBi
body_text:
Hello All,
We are working on building an intranasal model for brain absorption by bypassing the Blood brain barrier through first order kinetics. Our rationale is that a portion of the drug will be absorbed directly to the brain, and some to the systemic circulation, the lungs system and to the digestive tract. We have applied the steps suggested by Pavel in [https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/MoBi/issues/246] to each of the compartments neighborhood (brain, systemic circulation, lung and Stomach) and to passive transports. we have used the following formula to denote dN/dT=Total_drug_massKa-Total_drug_massKloss. We have created a new event for the administration of the drug as a bolus as the image show below. When we ran the simulation we received this error stating that the plasma concentration is negative. We have tried to use the equation -K*concentration. but the error remained. I would like to know were the error was and to make sure our equations are correct.
Appreciate all help!
Mahmoud

title: Pregnancy/Fetus model Aging
body_text:
Hello,
I was trying to use the Dallmann 2017 Pregnancy Model to simulate daily administration to a woman over an entire 9 months pregnancy. And I was initially assuming that it should be possible to dynamically take into account the physiological/anatomical pregnancy related ageing/maturation processes in the simulation. However, this seems not to be possible. When creating a Pregnant PBPK Model in MoBi as outlined in the Dallmann 2018 Tutorial, the parameters in the pregnant woman spatial structure are static, so do not include dynamic ageing.
Can somebody confirm that what I intended is not possible?
And finally, isnt the information required for this present in the PK Sim Population database? So shouldnt it in principle be possible to do include these physiol/anatomical ageing/maturation processes?

answer.body_text:
No, unfortunately a "growing" pregnancy population is currently not possible, although all information is in the database.
I created a feature request for that a long time ago, if I find it again, I will post it here so that it gets more support from the community ;-)

title: How can I define a non-enzymatic hydrolysis?
body_text:
Hi everybody,
The drug I seek to model undergoes non-enzymatic hydrolysis and I need to include the metabolite (product from hydrolysis), which is cleared by renal filtration, in the model as well.
Is there a way to include this process in the default model? If not, how can this be done using Moby?
Thank you very much!
best regards,
Oliver

answer.body_text:
Hi Oliver,
Certainly, however, you would still implement this with a dummy enzyme uniformly expressed across all compartments/tissues where hydrolysis might happen. You can then link the parent and metabolite model through this dummy enzyme and define a renal CL for the metabolite.
Best,
Stephan

title: Food Effect?
body_text:
I have poked around a bit and have a rough understanding of how food affects pk-sim models, but I have been unable to find the actual equations incorporating the affect and thus have been unable to really understand what's going on.
I tried looking at the Matlab equations and couldn't find it, but maybe I missed something?
Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated. Thanks.
DS

answer.body_text:
Basically, the food intake specified in the Events building block triggers changes in changes in gastric pH (increases to 5.5 then decays exponentially), gastric emptying time (prolonged), and stomach lumen volume (increased by the fluid volume of the ingested meal).
You can see these equations when you export your model to MoBi and navigate to the Events building block under Meal_StartEvent in the section of your meal intake. See e.g. here
See also this discussion here which might help you.

title: Fitting endosomal clearance parameter for large molecules
body_text: I am currently in the process of making a large molecule drug model. However, currently it seems like the endosomal clearance of the drug is a bit too low, based on the observed data that I have. I can either increase the rate constant for endosomal uptake, or decrease the rate constant for recycling from the endosomal space. If we assume that different large molecules have different endosomal clearance, which one of the two parameters would I adjust to make it more physiologically based? Does it make more sense to say more of the drug enters the endosome, or does it make more sense to say less of it gets recycled?

comments.body_text:
Hi @akim2000, how does the observed data inform endosomal CL? Did you specify a Kd for FcRn binding? This Kd defines the fraction recycled. As it is a drug-specific parameter, it makes sense to adjust the Kd. But if you left it at default ( very large number) it is already at maximum.
Do you think there could be other CL pathways?
Best,
Stephan

title: very high confidence interval of parameter estimates
body_text:
Hi there,
I get this very big confidence interval for the parameter estimates. I wonder what does that say about my model. Also, I'm a bit confused about how did Mobi manage to calculate a confidence interval when everything is so far individual (mean data with error bar)

Lots of thanks in advance!

comments.body_text: Also, what is the difference between confidence interval, visual predictive check interval, prediction interval, that plots for parameter estimation?

hi @hainiwen these confidence intervals are calculated based on the uncertainty of your parameter estimation, i.e. how much does the total error change if you change the estimated parameter value. The very large intervals indicate your data is not so informative to estimate these parameters precisely. The confidence interval graph uses these uncertainties in the parameter values to calculated how this translates into uncertainties of predicted output values of your model. The VPC calculates the standard deviation of the distribution of the residuals between observed data and model predictions, and uses that SD to calculate an interval around the predicted output values, per definition this interval covers 95% of the observed data, so that does not inform you on the goodness of fit, but you could use it to identify datapoints that are far out of the predicted interval. the prediction interval, finally, adds these two variabilities to predict the range in which you would expect future data to appear.

title: There are some problems with running the results after incorporating the math formula
body_text:
Hello, everyone,
I'm creating math equations in mobi, but the results are a little different from what I envisioned, I'll briefly describe my problem with an example.
I added a formula with the following parameters:
A: Dimension: Fraction, Value: 6.3
B: Dimension: Time, Value: 18.48 h
C: Dimension: Dimensionless, Formula: A×cos((2×pi/24)×(Time-B))


I believe that when I set it up this way, "C" is only related to "A", "B" and "Time", and any other parameters should not affect "C".
Plot C, The result is plotted below:

The results were not what I expected, and I even thought they were "wrong".
I used Excel to generate some values and I went to graphing and the results are as follows:

This result should be what I want.
I don't know why I get this result, is it due to defining a different "Dimension"? Do we need to define the unit before using "Time" as a variable? Or do I need to define all "Dimensions" as "Dimensionless"?

answer.body_text:
You must be aware that all entities are converted to the base OSP units for calculations.
Base unit for the time dimension is [min]. So your formulas evaluates to
A×cos((2×pi/24)×(Time_in_Minutes-18.48*60))

title: Administer Multiple Doses in R
body_text:
How can I manipulate a simulation in R so that I can administer multiple doses for a given drug using the R ospsuite package?
For example, I want to administer a dose of 5mg and 10mg of Drug A and drug B respectively, at time 0, and 5mg of drug A at 30 minutes, and 7 mg of drug B at 60 minutes.
I couldn't figure out how to do it here.
Any help would be much appreciated.
Thanks,
DS

answer.body_text:
no problem.
see
https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/reference/getAllParametersMatching.html
and
see https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/set-values.html
# Get objects representing the molecule Aciclovir in all containers
allAciclovirMolecules <- getAllMoleculesMatching("Organism|**|Aciclovir", sim)

# Set initial values to 10 µmol in all containers
setMoleculeInitialValues(allAciclovirMolecules, rep(10, length(allAciclovirMolecules)))

the same methods exist for parameters. You get an array when using wildcard. Very very cool

comments.body_text:
Hi @DonkeySaddle,
I believe its not possible to add dosing events in R yet. What you can do is add the number of dosing events that you maximally want to apply in your simulations to your model in PK-Sim/MoBi, and set the dose of some of these dosing events to 0 in R if you want to simulate less dosing events.

answer.body_text:
Cellular permeability calculation did not change between versions.
Looking at your screenshots, it seems that
a) either you are using different calculation methods for cellular permeability (e.g. "Standard" in 11.0 and "Schmitt" in 11.2)
b) or you are working with different compound properties in 11.0 and 11.2 (e.g. added/modified pKa values)

title: Protein model parameters calculated from MW
body_text:
Dear all,
I have a question about the use of the MW in the calculations in the protein model.
I had a look at the structure of the model and according to my understanding the MW is only used to calculate two parameters in the protein model (considering a large molecule administered IV):

Radius
Aqueous diffusion coefficient

For this reason, two compounds, independently of their MW, should generate the same simulation if these two parameters are the same. I did this test and, the simulations are different (I created two compounds with different MW and mixed their respective parameters).
Considering these results, it seems that the MW itself has an impact on the simulations. Does anybody have an explanation of this effect? I guess I am overlooking something.
I have attached the file with a dummy example I created.
Thank you in advance for your help!
Best,
Donato
Dummy test calculation MW protein model.zip

answer.body_text:
Hi Donato,
you are right with, the same radius (and Kd(FcRn)) the proteins should behave the same (aqueous diffusion coefficient is relevant for particle dissolution, i.e. not part of the protein model). The difference in in your simulations stems from using the same mg-based dose. With the different molecular weights, the dose amount in µmol is different, thus the factor 2 difference in the simulated profiles (µmol/l).
Best, Christoph

you are right with, the same radius (and Kd(FcRn)) the proteins should behave the same (aqueous diffusion coefficient is relevant for particle dissolution, i.e. not part of the protein model). With the different molecular weights, the dose amount in µmol is different, thus the factor 2 difference in the simulated profiles (µmol/l).

title: Different CYP2C19 expression database methods resulted in completely different PK profiles of a drug.
body_text:
Hi there,
I am trying to develop a generic PBPK model for a compound that is majorly metabolized by CYP2C19. I have noticed that changing the CYP2C19 expression database (i.e., from RT-PCR to array) resulted in a significantly different PK profile of the compound (See attached graph for reference). With RT-PCR- the profile is overestimated when compared to the observed data and using the array method resulted in an underprediction of PK profile.
Hence, I was wondering if one of the expression database methods has an advantage over the others. If not, How do we make a decision on which method to use as this will have a significant impact on the parameter optimization?

Thank You,
Sahithi

title: Is there a way to observe the proportion of the transporter in the elimination process?
body_text: Excuse me, is there a way in PK-sim to be able to observe the proportion of the transporter in the elimination process?

title: Terminal half life of midazolam in PBPK model
body_text:
Hi Everyone,
The terminal half life of midazolam is very short. Due to how PK Sim calculates the half life, I get wrong half lifes (very long ~30h) out of my simulations.
This is how PK-Sim calculates the half life: Terminal half life time (calculated from the terminal 10% of data points).
I tried to change the sampling data points and the time till sampling, but this does not work very well.
What is the right way to simulate compounds/drugs with a very short half life to get the right AUC_inf, Cmax and T1/2?
Thank you!
Marjolein

comments.body_text:
Hi Marjolein,
I read you question and would like to ask you whether you could find a way how to influence half-life value in PK Sim Simulations? I would be very happy to discuss this topic with you.
We try to get used to PK Sim and it is a common problem with our simulations, that half-life is too long as it should be.
We tried to chance logP and partition coefficient and cellular permeability, but it was not very successful.
Thank you and I am looking forward to hearing from you.
Best, Johanna

Hi Johanna,

Thank you for contacting me! I am afraid I have to disappoint you. Due to lack of time, I temporarily stopped using and learning PK Sim and switched to Simcyp. (My research group has more experience with that platform).
However, I can advise you to put your problem on the Github forum with specifics on which model you use. I really quickly got a response by one of the PK Sim experts.

My question was focussed on that I want to use t1/2 as PK parameter to validate my PBPK models (together with Cmax and AUC). They only responded they don't advise t1/2, since it is calculated by the last 10% of data points of the terminal slope. With this in mind, the problem of the long half-life might be caused if the drug is not completely eliminated in the simulation or another factor why the last part of the curve is not correctly.

I am really interested in the solution. I hope to pick up my work in PK Sim in a couple of months and will ask a colleague who recently started to use PK Sim if he has problems with t1/2.  If I figure out the problem, I am happy to discuss this with you. I hope you will find the solution.

Best,
Marjolein

Hi Stephan,

thank you very much for you reply.
That`s a great idea to run a sensitivity analysis. We haven’t done it yet.
I would like to have one more question about your diagram. Am I interpreting correctly that the parameter that deviates the most from zero has the highest influence on in our example, the half-life?

We simulate cytarabine given IV for 3 hours. Cytarabine has very shot half-life, initial only 10 minutes and our simulation has more than 60 hours:
***@***.***

In our simulation, we changed LogP so that Cmax matched our target. As the next step, we changed the hepatic clearance. That helped to reduce the half-life and to improve the AUC matching the target, but our Cmax was changed too and did not matched the target any more.
Do you know  the reason for such behavior of PK-Sim?

Thank you very much for you help!

Best, Johanna

Betreff: Re: [Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Forum] Terminal half life of midazolam in PBPK model (Discussion #1488)


Hi Johanna,

Have you tried running a sensitivity analysis for t1/2 to find the most influential parameters? That's what you get for the "Chung 2006" PO simulation in the template model:
[image]<https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25477643/271958780-4ba98a9e-e925-4b09-9577-b28b8770c4aa.png>
Here half-life is 2.4 h.

What type of simulation are you running, IV or PO? How long are you simulating (long enough to actually get into terminal clearance (> 6 hours))?
Half-life sensitivity might depend on what clearance processes are implemented. For Midazolam, we have liver and kidney, so you might want to look at Vmax for CYP3A4 or parameters for glomerular filtration.

Best,
Stephan

title: Molecule concentration in MoBi is not zero also if the molecule is not expressed
body_text:
Dear all,
I am having an issue with MoBi V11 which I am not able to solve.
I needed to create a tumor compartment in MoBi with a target expressed.
These are the steps I followed:
-I created the target in PK-Sim and expressed it in the muscle
-I exported the model in MoBi and cloned the muscle to create the tumor compartment
-I follow the usual steps to create a tumor compartment. The simulation runs correctly.
-I then wanted to take away the target in the muscle and expressed it in the tumor. I first changed the relative expression and set it to 1 in the tumor. Now the target concentrations appeared in the tumor and in the muscle. Then I set up the relative expression in the muscle to 0 but in the simulation, I still see the target appearing.
The building blocks are updated, the simulation as the right reference concentration (0) and I also tried to configure again the simulation but still the same problem.
Could you tell me if is there any other parameter that could explain why the target still appears in the muscle?
Thanks in advance for your help!
Best,
Donato

comments.body_text:
Hi Donato,
I recently added a tumor with in V11 and it worked without problems (also regarding expression on the organ which was cloned).
Maybe you set "Relative expression in vascular endothelium" > 1? Where is your target supposed to be localized within tissue?
Best, Christoph

@teutonicod

When you click on the "Extend" button in the upper left corner, the entry for the parameter of your new molecule will appear there.

title: How to create an observer that calculates total amount of drug that goes into a compartment
body_text:
I am currently trying to create a brain model and need an observer to ensure that whatever changes I make to my model isn't somehow messing up the entire mass balance (i.e., I want observers to show me that: (total drug going into brain) = (total drug leaving brain + drug remaining in the brain + complexes formed)). But before looking at both sides, I wanted to focus on the first half, which is total drug going into the brain.
From my knowledge, I'm pretty sure that all of the drug that reaches the brain (or any other organs) should be coming from the arterial blood compartment with the passive transport 'MassTransferBloodPool2OrgPl' (correct me if I am wrong). If so, I was wondering if it was possible to somehow observe and calculate the total amount of drug that is coming into the brain, so that I can compare with the second part of my equation (total drug leaving brain + drug remaining in the brain + complexes formed). How would I create an observer for this situation, or is there a better way to check that my mass balance isn't accidently messed up?

comments.body_text:
You should also consider the drug transported with blood cells, this would be the passive transport (PT) MassTransferBloodPool2OrgRBC.
By enabling the checkbox "Create process rate parameter", you can have access to the rate of the transport (you can do the same for reactions btw):



Then you can create a "State variable parameter" (I think this does not work with observers), e.g. in the brain plasma (under MoleculeProperties), called e.g. TotalAmountInflow, mark it as "Parameter is state variable", and define its right hand side as the process rate of the inflow passive transport. Tha path to the process rate would be Neighborhoods|ArterialBlood_pls_Brain_pls|MOLECULE|MassTransferBloodPool2OrgPl|ProcessRate

Then do the same for inflow with the RBC, and for the outflows. Keep in mind, that if you are working with the model for large molecules, there is an additional outflow process via lymph and some endosomal degradation that you should consider in the mass balance.
Where/how would I view the total drug inflow after creating the parameters?

title: Choice of Algorithm and Site of Enzymatic Metabolism
body_text:
Hi all,
For my graduation project, I am working on a hydrophilic drug that is mainly renally secreted and undergoes minimal metabolism prior to excretion. I implemented basolateral and apical transporters and tested an intracellular enzyme at different sites. I have done simultaneous optimizations to estimate parameters using DDI with probenecid. Best fit of plasma concentration and fractions excreted in urine was obtained with the following localizations: renal and renal + hepatic.
I tried Levenberg, Monte-Carlo and multiple optimizations.
I have a couple of questions:
What algorithm is preferred for optimization in this case?
Should I select the localization based on the lowest error, or should I prioritize physiological plausibility?
Your replies are highly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

title: Target Controlled Infusion
body_text:
My name is Lucas Wamser, and I am a veterinarian working on a research team in Brazil. We recently developed a PBPK model for a specific anesthetic in dogs. We are now interested in using this model to control an infusion pump.
To do this, we would like to know if there is a way to define a target plasma concentration and if the model could calculate the infusion rates needed to maintain this concentration over time. Doing this would be the opposite of the conventional procedure since we usually set a dose, and the model calculates the resulting concentration based on it.
Can we accomplish this using the OSPsuit package, Javascript, PK-Sim, or MObi? There may be another way.
Would anyone have the knowledge or experience to help us solve this issue?
Thank you for any guidance or assistance you can offer.

comments.body_text:
If you have a predefined infusion schema (e.g. new infusion starts every 15 minutes or so), you could use a Parameter Identification Tool in PK-Sim or MoBi for doing this.
You can import your target concentration as an observed data set with constant concentration, and then optimize doses and infusion times for each administration.
Example parameter identification with 3 infusions every 8 hours


Comparison of the concentration with the target concentration over 24 hours

If you have a free infusion schema (infusion pump starts "on demand"), it becomes a more challenging task.
You still could solve this task with a predefined infusion schema (where infusions are defined very frequently) - however, the parameter optimization might not deliver good results in this case.
Other solutions via events definition in MoBi, more sophisticated optimization via R etc. could be developed as well.

title: What should be considered about unknown metabolic ratios?
body_text:
Hello everyone.
I know the proportion of enzyme metabolism contribution of a drug and the fraction excreted unchanged in urine. These fractions add up to a value of 80%. But in the software simulation, the sum of the enzyme metabolism contribution ratio and the fraction excreted to urine is 100%. I wonder how the unknown 20% should be considered? Is it possible to convert to 100% by a co-multiplication factor? Or is it possible to add total hepatic clearance and attribute that 20% here?

comments.body_text:
Dear Chelton,
hm, a bit hard to answer without knowing more details. I would tend to not use total hepatic clearance but instead to add an enzyme in liver and name unspecific process or so. Than it is easier to calculate the massbalance in the simulation again. B.t.w. did you think about that the missing 20 % in your known massbalance might be not absorbed compound in case it was po administration?
Best, Tobias

Therefore, we decided to represent this fraction of other products as "total hepatic clearance".

title: Update on gene expression databases including pre-clinical and animal species
body_text:
Dear open-systems-pharmacology community,
we are currently working on an update of the gene expression databases of PK-Sim.
To this end we're preparing a publication that will outline the database generation, the R-Code that creates them and the first release the of the databases. Ideally we could include them in one of the next releases and make the scripts part of the git repository, let's see.
It will still take a while, but nevertheless we wanted to keep you updated and a first glimpse of what you can expect within 2022.
As requested in previous posts #1125 #1069 #882 #864, the next line of gene expression databases will cover various animal species relevant in pre-clincial research, animal healthy and toxicology, as well as an update on the human data.
Currently we cover the following species:
Human, Monkey (Macaca mulatta & Macaca fascicularis), Minipig, Dog, Rat, Mouse, Rabbit, Zebrafish, Cattle, Horse, Cat, Guinea Pig, Chicken, Turkey, Goat and Sheep
All gene annotations are made with the biomaRt - Bioconductor package and are based on ensemble and NCBI gene identifiers.
For animal species, the databases include a link to the human homolog, such that you can query for example for a human CYP3A4 and the database suggests the animal homologs.
The source data is based generated from RNAseq experiments derived from healthy primary tissue samples gathered from public sources. No cell line or disease data is included. Note that the databases also do not include protein abundance information.
I'm happy to present you the beta versions of Rat and Rabbit. Feel free to check them out!
Let me know if you find bugs or have any suggestions, I'm happy to include them in the next release.
GENEDB_rat_TPM_ONLY_BgeeRelease_14_0_2022-01-26.zip
GENEDB_rabbit_TPM_ONLY_BgeeRelease_14_1_2022-01-26.zip
Kind regards,
Henrik
@sfrechen PS: Based on your request and suggestion that this will be most interesting for others,  I put together the human database.
So here it is, fresh out of the press:
GENEDB_human_ADME_ONLY_TPM_ONLY_BgeeRelease_14_0_2022-02-15.zip
PSPS: Please note, this beta-version for human data contains ADME genes only  and is based only on a fraction of the available data for example not including data form the "Genotype-Tissue Expression (GTEx) Common Fund Project" and other viable source that might influence the overall expression pattern.

comments.body_text: This is fantastic! What an amazing contribution. Thank you.
Great contribution @HenrikCordes, thanks a lot!
Excellent! Amazing work!!!
Yes, a great piece of work! Thanks Henrik
Thanks a lot! I will be looking forward to the dog database.
@HenrikCordes Yes. Can you create an issue in PKSim repositories for this specific request? I think we need to track this separately

Hi Community,
as this was requested during the PAGE, please find attatched the Sheep ADME gene expression DB:
GENEDB_sheep_ADME_ONLY_TPM_ONLY_BgeeRelease_15_0_2022-07-23.zip
Kind regards,
Henrik

title: Transfer of PBPK model between versions
body_text:
Hi,
I would like to transfer all the PBPK models I'm working on in version 9 of PK Sim to version 11.0. Therefore, I exported the models to snapshot, and loaded them in v11.0.
For most medicines, this resulted in very similar results when I compared the predictions. However, for metformin the predicted plasma concentration is shifted downwards and the total plasma clearance is higher. In version 9, I have been working further on the model for metformin as developed by Nina Hanke. I already noticed that the Vmax of some transporters was not taken over when uploading the snapshot in version 11, but even after correcting this there is a difference between the versions. When I compare the AUC and Cmax for each simulation between version 9 and 11, there seems to be an equal shift downwards in all simulations (see figure where each point represents one simulation).

I'm wondering what the reason for this difference is? Is there a difference between the versions that can explain this observation, or should I look further for parameters that were not taken over correctly (so far I did not observe any differences)?
In addition, I also noticed that the 'number of repetitions' and 'time between repetitions' in the advanced administration protocol were not always taken over correctly when I used snapshot to transfer PBPK models between versions. I'm not sure if this issue has been fixed/reported already?

comments.body_text:
Hi @nina-nauwelaerts,
A first thing you might want to check is the (intracellular) concentration of the involved enzymes.
My understanding is that the calculation method of expression levels has been changed between these versions.
Some information is provided here for the v10.0 release: https://github.com/Open-Systems-Pharmacology/Suite/releases
Others might be able to provide more detail on this. A related discussion is located here: #1151
Hope you can find the cause of this discrepancy!

title: Why doesn't the amount/concentration of CYP3A4 in the Liver Intracellular compartment change throughout my simulation?
body_text:
Plots are flat (though nonzero) and unmoving? I understand the amount of
CYP3A4  could be so large, that the drug doesn't have an impact, but given that there is an interaction by way of competitive inhibition with CYP3A4, I assumed the amount of CYP3A4 would change.

answer.body_text:
Competitive inhibition should not alter enzyme concentration (Km changes due to larger amounts of substrate being needed to reach the same reaction velocity when another compound is competing for binding to the reaction site).
In PKSim, only irreversible (time-dependent) inhibition and induction impact the actual enzyme concentrations (by respectively increasing degradation and prouction), which you would see in the CYP-concentration-time plots. You can easily try this out yourself by implementing one of these interactions.
The underlying principles are explained nicely in the manual and supporting references: https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-compounds-defining-inhibition-induction-processes
Good luck!

title: PK Simulation of Bortezomib - half-life adjustment
body_text:
Dear all,
I am on creating of simulation of Bortezomib in PK-Sim and faced few problems which I would like to discuss with you.


With which parameter in PK-Sim could I adjust half-life of the compond?


I created two simulations, one for dose 1 mg/m2 and one for dose 1.3 mg/m2. To match Cmax in plasma (from literature) which are belonging to these different doses two different LogPs are needed. How is that possible?


Thank you for your opinions and help.
Johanna

comments.body_text:
Hi @JohannaZH,
You can use different parameters, for example the lipophilicity as you mentioned. You could also try different calculation methods for the partition coefficients and the cellular permeability. Apart from that, hepatic clearance and renal clearance would be the most logical options to start with, I would think, although I am not aware of the specifics for Bortezomib.
For you second question, first have a look at the non-linearity of you Cmax, is it indeed not proportional to the dose? If not, you could think about saturable clearance, saturable metabolism in the gut or saturable absorption mechanisms.
Hope this helps, you still have lots of options to explore it seems!
Wilbert

title: Questions regarding fraction of drug recycled and passive transport of drug
body_text:
I had two questions regarding how PK-Sim (MoBi) defines some of their parameters and formulas.


When looking at the fraction of drug recycled to the plasma relative to the interstitial space, PK sim has FR = 1. However, I believe literature states the FR to be 0.715 (Chang HY, Wu S, Meno-Tetang G, Shah DK. A translational platform PBPK model for antibody disposition in the brain. J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn. 2019 Aug 15;46(4):319–38. ) I was wondering why PK-Sim uses FR = 1.



When looking at the passive transport system for NetMassTransfer_EndosomalToPlasma_FcRn_Complex, we can see that the source of the volume used in the calculation is the same as the the source of the concentration (ie. it uses concentration of molecule in the endosome multiplied by volume of the endosome to calculated the amount of FcRn complex present).



However, when we look at NetMassTransfer_PlasmaToEndosomal, it uses the concentration of the source multiplied by the volume of the target, not the volume of the source (ie. C_pls* V_end, instead of C_pls*V_pls). In order to calculate the amount of drug present in the plasma, shouldn't the formula use the volume of the source container?

comments.body_text:
Hi @akim2000,
To start with your last question, this is actually correct, but it might appear initially to be a mistake, here is the reason:
the uptake rate constant (kup) that is used for unbound drug and endogenous IgG is not the same as kup for FcRn and FcRn bound molecules: their ratio is given by the V_pls/V_end ratio. This makes sense, as a smaller fraction of the circulating unbound drug is captured in comparison to the fraction of membrane-bound FcRn when an endosome is formed from the membrane. However, the same rate constant K_uptake is used in PK-Sim for both processes.
For the unbound drug equation kup is K_uptake * Vend/Vpls. If you multiply this with Vpls as you suggest and is the correct thing to do, the equation can be simplified to K_uptake * Vend, as used in PK-Sim.
For the FcRn molecule kup is the same as K_uptake, so K_uptake * Vpls would be appropriate there, but for some reason I dont fully understand, PK-Sim uses  f_mem_pls * K_uptake *  V_end = Vend/Vpls * K_uptake * Vend = Vpls/Vend * K_uptake * Vend/Vpls * Vpls = K_uptake * Vpls
With respect to the reason of chosing a FR of 0.75 vs 1, I believe PK-Sim uses 1 for reasons of identifiability, and a clearer separation of the two-pore distribution model and the FcRn-mediated salvaging from clearance model.
Most of this is described in the original publication of this model: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5845054/
Part of the additions above is also described in our recent publication: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36877385/
Hopefully this helps!
Best,
Wilbert

Hi,
To your 1. question: In different published PBPK models, different values for this fraction are used. PK-Sim is not the only model using recycling to plasma only. See the paper  https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-017-9559-4 for different uptake/recycling fractions used in PBPK models and respective citations. At the end, the values are not well identifiable using the usual data for fitting. With the value used in PK-Sim, there is no transcytosis via the endosome, i.e. the two-pore mechanisms is the only mechanism responsible for extravasation. The current parameterisation was chosen to clearly separate the mechanism for extravasation described by the two-pore equation and the endosomal clearance/FcRn mediated recycling. Structurally it is possible to use different fractions for the case there are better data allowing to quantify them.
To you 2. question:
The uptake mechanism into the endosome is pinocytosis, i.e. a certain fluid volume is endocytosed into the cell and the same fluid volume is going back (to e.g. plasma, see above) to maintain cell volume. The equations in PK-Sim are formulated in a way to describe this fluid volumes as a fraction of endosomal volume (fraction being implicitly present in rate constants).
Hope that helps.

title: Problems with creating new container for brain
body_text:
I am currently trying to implement a blood-brain barrier for a large molecule model for the brain. I have currently removed the existing endosome compartment and created a 'BBB' compartment in the place of it (will add CSF-related stuff later but wanted to test adding compartments first). I created all of the neighborhoods and passive transports required for this compartment to work as intended (ie. right now I just wanted it to work like the pre-existing endosome compartment, but just separated from the endogenous IgG and FcRn system in PK-Sim, as I am planning on creating a specific FcRn system just for the brain compartment). As a reference, I added some other organs to see if they were acting weird, but the rest seemed to be fine. I was wondering what could possibly be the cause of this as the shape of the curve seems to be normal for both lines? Any suggestions would be helpful.

answer.body_text: Actually, I have figured out that it was most likely the passive transport from plasma to BBB that was way too fast.

title: Reduce Tolerances using ospsuite R
body_text: Hi, I am running batch simulations with the R package and sometimes observe an error when performing a large number of simulations. Performing the simulations in R and PK-Sim do not produce errors, neither do small batches, and I am also sure that my input ranges are appropriate. I noticed that there has been discussion concerning this in the past, including "allowing negative values" via Mobi and a possible auto-reduce tolerance feature in the future, however I could not find information on reducing the tolerances within R. Is is possible to do so?

answer.body_text:
Yes, if your simulation is "sim" [sim = loadSimulation(simFilePath)]
then e.g.:
sim$solver$absTol = 1e-10
sim$solver$relTol = 1e-7

title: how to allow some compartments to have negative concentrations
body_text:
Hi there!
I have some error here.
While I am pretty sure that the mass transfer should be right, as the concentrations are quite low, I cannot compute the current model with a Tolerance about 1E-9
When I set it as Tol 1E-4, it works but the concentration curves are quite bumpy when it goes below 1E-5.
So I was wondering, can I keep the Tolerance, but allow negative values in some compartments. I heard it's do-able, but I don't know where is the setting...
Thank you in advance.
Haini

answer.body_text:
You can change the setting for each output in the Molecule Start Values building block and then update your simulation.
After that, you should plot all the curves which were identified as having negative values before and check if the negative values are acceptable (can be considered as numerical noise) or not.
If the values are not acceptable: either there is something wrong with the model or the solver settings must be adjusted.

comments.body_text:
There are several potential reasons, hard to say without further details of what you have done.
Possible reasons are e.g.:

Missing neighborhoods between your new compartments and the previously present one.
The passive transport process which is supposed to be responsible for getting drug  into the new compartments to not recognize the new compartments as target.

For the concepts you could look at: https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-mobi/mobi-documentation/building-block-concepts

title: How to set the ADME parameter when you only know: the proportion of the drug prototype and the metabolites metabolized by CYP3A4 in the urine？

title: What should I do when applying two models for DDI simulations that use different enzyme databases?
